title: [Windows] Maximizing borderless ignore window maximum size
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible: v4.5.beta7.official [4ebf67c12], v4.5.beta6.official [d5ad0556a], v4.5.beta5.official [c81fd6c51]

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-7600K CPU @ 3.80GHz (4 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

When maximizing a window borderless, it will always use all usable rectangle which is not the same behaviour for a non-borderless window.  

Note: I don't know which one is right, probably the non-borderless.  

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/894ecc4c-4cb1-4c8b-865c-072cca1a96a5

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	get_window().max_size = Vector2(500, 500)
	get_window().min_size = Vector2(200, 200)
	get_window().borderless = true
	get_window().mode = Window.MODE_MAXIMIZED
```

Commenting the `get_window().borderless = true` will show the behaviour when non-borderless.

title: MSVC builds are broken for 4.5 (825ef23)
body:
### Tested versions

825ef23

### System information

Windows 11, Intel® Core™ i5-10300H, Nvidia GTX 1650 (552.44)

### Issue description

On the master branch, when attempting to build the engine from source using Microsoft Visual C++ compiler `Microsoft (R) C/C++ version 19.40.33813 for x86`
with the command:
`scons platform=windows target=editor arch=x86_64`

the build fails with the following errors:
```
.\core/templates/self_list.h(123): error C2327: 'SelfList<CellData>::_self' is not a type, static member, or enumerator
.\core/templates/self_list.h(123): note: see declaration of 'SelfList<CellData>::_self'
.\core/templates/self_list.h(123): note: in the context of instantiating a template (first instantiated here)
scene\2d\tile_map_layer.cpp(294): note: template function instantiation "void SelfList<CellData>::List::sort_custom<CellDataYSortedXReversedComparator>()" being compiled
scene\2d\tile_map_layer.cpp(294): note: first referenced here in "TileMapLayer::_rendering_update"
.\core/templates/self_list.h(123): error C2327: 'SelfList<CellData>::_prev' is not a type, static member, or enumerator
.\core/templates/self_list.h(123): note: see declaration of 'SelfList<CellData>::_prev'
.\core/templates/self_list.h(123): error C2327: 'SelfList<CellData>::_next' is not a type, static member, or enumerator
.\core/templates/self_list.h(123): note: see declaration of 'SelfList<CellData>::_next'
scons: *** [bin\obj\scene\2d\tile_map_layer.windows.editor.x86_64.obj] Error 2
scons: building terminated because of errors.
```
The relevant line in [self_list.h](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/core/templates/self_list.h) (line 123) is:
`SortList<Element, T *, &Element::_self, &Element::_prev, &Element::_next, PtrComparator> sorter;`

It appears that commit 6b2674f breaks MSVC builds. Building the 4.3 branch does not produce this issue.

### Steps to reproduce

N/A

N/A

title: Crash when synthetic and native mouse clicks are sent over a visible Control
body:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6]
v4.5.beta7.official [4ebf67c12]

also tested with gdscript and it crashes as well

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Fedora Linux 42 (Adams) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 SUPER (nvidia; 575.64.05) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4790K CPU @ 4.00GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Game crashes with no error message when certain rapid synthetic mouse events (via Input.ParseInputEvent) are sent while native mouse clicks occur over a visible Control.

Specifically, it is reliably reproduced when quickly clicking left and right mouse buttons in the MRP *inside* the area of the control.

The crash does not occur if the Control is invisible, and it only triggers when both synthetic and native clicks interact with the Control.
You can work around the crash by adding metadata to your synthetic events and doing something like ```if (!ev.HasMeta("virtual_mouse")) GetViewport().SetInputAsHandled();```. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a duplicate of mouse event. Add metadata such as "virtual_mouse" to prevent infinite recursion.
2. Add a control to the scene. Resize it to fill some of the screen.
3. Rapidly click the left and right mouse buttons within the Control area.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[synethtic-click-crash-mrp-gdscript.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22061531/synethtic-click-crash-mrp-gdscript.zip)

title: Script editor doesn't jump to the first line after calling `EditorInterface::edit_script()`
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.beta7.official [4ebf67c12]

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7 - Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm) 12 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon R7 360 Series (RADV BONAIRE) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3450 CPU @ 3.10GHz (4 threads) - 7.69 GiB memory

### Issue description

When calling `EditorInterface::edit_script()` with `line` argument set to 1, the script editor doesn't jump to the first line. It works correctly with line 2 or higher, but not line 1.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open MRP
2. Open `node_3d.tscn` scene
3. Click on the root node
4. In the inspector, click "Jump to line" tool button
5. Switch to the script editor
6. In the script editor, change the caret position to a line other than 1
7. Click "Jump to line" tool button again

Expected behavior:
The caret jumps to the first line of the script

Current behavior:
The caret stays on the same line as before

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[edit-script-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22066471/edit-script-test.zip)
author: fstxz

title: IME preedit apostrophe (U+0027) leaks into committed text → multiple regressions in editor since 4.3/4.4 (TextEdit caret error; Find-in-Files desync; apostrophes between letters)
body:
# Tested Versions

###  Observed issues

**① Script Editor (TextEdit)**  
---

Chinese IME composition can be interrupted (✅ expected),  
but after interruption, the **pinyin syllable separator `'` (U+0027)** is mistakenly committed as normal text.  
e.g. typing *fenhao* produces `fen'hao`.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a1d73159-e0aa-4a26-8ca3-1db6e1c45fab


**② “Find in Files” search field**  
---

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8d9a3019-d28c-48d4-a92a-2b16340a818d

After IME interruption,  `'` remains.  
After interruption, visible Latin letters **aren’t recognized by search** until the user edits once.



**③ Error flood below "#" (later confirmed to be hover-related)**  
---

Below the "#", Typing Chinese in a script comment and interrupting it leaves pinyin and triggers **a flood of errors** (especially in 4.5.dev5).  
Later investigation confirmed this was **actually caused by IME text retrieval during mouse hover**,  
and it was fully fixed by [PR #111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859).  
✅ **Case③ fixed.**

**③-b Hover flood after parser error (follow-up observation)
---

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/07773610-0e4f-4015-8483-258c75f83a9e

After a Parser Error, hovering the mouse over the affected line often triggers a flood of errors:

`ERROR: scene\gui\code_edit.cpp:2035 - Condition "p_column - 1 > get_line(p_line).size()" is true. Returning: Point2(-1, -1)`

See the reproduction steps below.

Steps to Reproduce case③-b:
---
STEP1: Open Godot and create a new GDScript file.

STEP2: Type with the Chinese IME, interrupt the input to produce composed text containing apostrophes (for example: w'w'w'w).

STEP3: Wait for the usual minor errors to appear (these are not the focus here).

STEP4: Repeat step ② two or three times to make the bug more likely to trigger.

STEP5: Hover the mouse over the affected line.

---

 Reproducible in
---
| Version | Cases |
|----------|--------|
| 4.3 | ① |
| 4.4 / 4.4.1–4.5.dev4 | ① + ② |
| 4.5.dev5 | ① + ② + ③+③b (error flood) |
| 4.5.beta1–4.6.dev2 | ① + ② +③b(③ fixed by #111859) |
|4.6.dev(After #111859 merged)|①+②+③b|

**Not reproducible in:**  
4.0.stable–4.2.2 (and 3.x)

---

# System Information

- **OS:** Windows 10 / Windows 11  
- **IME:** Microsoft Pinyin / Sogou Pinyin

---

# Summary

Since **Godot 4.3**, the editor correctly allows **interrupting IME preedit** (✅ expected).  
However, when composition ends (usually via **mouse click** or **focus change**),  
the **pinyin separator `'`** is mistakenly treated as committed text.

---

| Area | Symptom | Note |
|------|----------|------|
| **Script Editor (TextEdit)** | Apostrophes `'` remain between Latin letters (`ni'hao`), caret position may desync. | Since 4.3 |
| **Find in Files** | Latin letters not recognized until edit; `'` inserted unexpectedly. | Since 4.4 |
| **Case③: Error flood** | Later confirmed hover-related, fixed by [#111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859). | 4.5.dev5 |

---

#  Root Cause (Technical)

Windows IME sends `WM_IME_COMPOSITION` with `GCS_RESULTSTR` when ending composition.  
Godot currently enqueues these characters (including `'`) into `_process_key_events()`,  
so `'` (U+0027) enters text as valid input, breaking caret and validation logic.

When `'` is filtered out during `ENDCOMPOSITION` and caret position is preserved,  
**Case① and Case② both disappear** (verified).

---

# ✅ Expected

- Apostrophe `'` should **not** appear in committed text.  

e.g
---

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/71f0ec29-ffe8-435a-a1f3-947d0f53e2e8

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f0863a43-2a66-479e-831b-c763bd483640

- TextEdit & Find in Files should **revalidate immediately** after IME ends.  
- No error flood when switching focus or hovering (Case③ fixed).

---

# ❌ Actual

- `'` remains (`ni'hao`).  
- Find in Files doesn’t recognize visible Latin letters.  
- (Old) Error flood on hover — fixed by [#111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859).

---

#  Notes

> Case② depends on Case① — once `'` stops leaking, Find in Files works normally.  
> Both belong to the same root cause (apostrophe leak at IME end).  
> Case③ turned out to be hover-related and was fixed by [#111859](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/111859).

---

 **In short:**  
Case① is the root cause; Case② is its chain reaction;  
Case③ looked related but was actually hover-handling, now fixed separately.
and Case③-b remains. (by the way,I test #100787  ,it seems  to fix both Case③ and Case③-b )

title: Font system fallback emoji missing important glyphs with Inter font
body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.5beta7 and 4.5beta5. ### System information MacOS and iOS (using #77951). Not tested on Windows ### Issue description Certain system fallback emoji characters don't render correctly, but only with the `Inter` font family. This issue is only important because Inter is one of the most widely used open source fonts. I can't seem to reproduce this behavior with any other font. When using the default font import settings that have `allow_system_fallback` enabled, all fonts but Inter seem to render the complete MacOS emoji set. Inter on-the-other-hand, will fallback to placeholder emoji for certain glyphs, like the red heart. If I manually set the fallbacks to Apple Color Emoji, Inter will render more glyphs, but not all of them, still failing on red heart, which sadly is one of the most needed emojis. "Warning" ⚠️ also fails - which is also a composite character like red heart. ### Steps to reproduce See MRP. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [font-issues-testing.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22067390/font-issues-testing.zip)

Windows 11

### Issue description

The VisibleOnScreenNotifier2D node is a useful tool for managing resources and logic based on a node's visibility. However, its screen_entered signal is not emitted if the node is already placed outside the camera's viewport when the scene starts.

The signal's logic seems to rely on a state transition (from "off-screen" to "on-screen"). Since a node starting outside the viewport doesn't undergo this initial transition, the signal is never triggered when the camera eventually moves to view it.

This behavior makes the node unsuitable for common game design patterns, such as:

Enemies or bosses that are placed in the scene tree but only become active when the player discovers them.

Lazy-loading content or physics areas that are far from the player's starting position.

Currently, the only workaround is to implement a manual visibility check using a rect intersection, which defeats the purpose of having a dedicated Notifier node.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new 2D scene.

2. Add a Camera2D with current enabled.

3. Add a CharacterBody2D and position it far outside the camera's initial view (e.g., at Vector2(1000, 0)).

4. Add a VisibleOnScreenNotifier2D as a child of the CharacterBody2D.

5. Attach a script to the CharacterBody2D. Connect the screen_entered signal from the Notifier to the script.

6. In the connected function, add a print("Entered screen!") statement.

7. Run the scene.

8. Move the camera towards the CharacterBody2D until it is fully visible on screen.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I will attach a minimal reproduction project (.zip) that clearly demonstrates this issue.

body:
### Tested versions

- v4.4.1.stable.mono.nixpkgs [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono (49a5bc7b6) - cpe:/o:nixos:nixos:25.11 #1-NixOS SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri Aug  1 08:48:47 UTC 2025 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon Graphics (radeonsi, renoir, ACO, DRM 3.61, 6.12.41) - AMD Ryzen 7 7730U with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

when running an exported project that was built on nixOS, on a non-nixOS linux system, the following fatal error is thrown (even with libicu installed):
```
Process terminated. Couldn't find a valid ICU package installed on the system. Please install libicu (or icu-libs) using your package manager and try again. Alternatively you can set the configuration flag System.Globalization.Invariant to true if you want to run with no globalization support. Please see https://aka.ms/dotnet-missing-libicu for more information.
   at System.Environment.FailFast(System.String)
   at System.Globalization.GlobalizationMode+Settings..cctor()
   at System.Globalization.CultureData.CreateCultureWithInvariantData()
   at System.Globalization.CultureData.get_Invariant()
   at System.Globalization.CultureInfo..cctor()
   at System.Globalization.CompareInfo..cctor()
   at System.CultureAwareComparer..cctor()
   at Internal.Runtime.InteropServices.ComponentActivator..cctor()
   at Internal.Runtime.InteropServices.ComponentActivator.LoadAssemblyAndGetFunctionPointer(IntPtr, IntPtr, IntPtr, IntPtr, IntPtr, IntPtr)
```

it is able to be worked-around by running the project with `DOTNET_SYSTEM_GLOBALIZATION_INVARIANT=1`, but its strange that this happens only when building from nixOS (i tested building from a linux mint VM, and it ran fine both within the VM and on my main nixOS system)

### Steps to reproduce

1. export (for linux) any c# project from a nixOS system
2. run that project on another non-nixOS linux system

title: "Create Collision Shape" operates on old version of a mesh when asset updates
body:
### Tested versions

 - Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7640) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900K CPU @ 3.60GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

Godot seems to operate on a cached version of the mesh data when Creating Collision Shapes from it, despite the mesh updating visually.

### Steps to reproduce

_This is done with a `.blend` automatic import_

1. Add a `.blend` to a scene
2. Right click on the new node and toggle "Editable Children" to see the mesh
    a. Optionally, you can create a New Inherited Scene and observe the same results
3. Select the child mesh
4. Open the Mesh menu and select "Create Collision Shape..."
5. Hit "Create" and observe that your `CollisionShape3D` is created
6. Open the file in Blender
7. Edit the mesh geometry & save
8. Return to Godot
9. Observe that all of your asset re-imports and all changes are visible in the scene as expected
10. Repeat steps 1 through 5 above
11. Observe that the new `CollisionShape3D` matches the **old** geometry, not matching your edits

I thought this might be intended (or some some other feature of an Inherited Scene that I'm not familiar with), but it  stops happening if you close and reopen Godot, so it seems like something is cached in-memory and isn't being updated the way the rest of the attributes get re-imported.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: `OS.get_processor_name`doesn't seem to work on iOS
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.beta7

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7 - iPhone XR - iOS 18.3.1

### Issue description

Put simply, calling `OS.get_processor_name` on iOS will seemingly always result in an error like so:

```
drivers/apple_embedded/os_apple_embedded.mm:419:get_processor_name(): Couldn't get the CPU model name. Returning an empty string. Method/function failed. Returning: ""
```

This seems to be due to `OS_AppleEmbedded::get_processor_name` relying on the `sysctl` name `machdep.cpu.brand_string`, which I believe only applies to macOS, but I could be wrong.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/52a5644b5f85eed80016c2dff905804e869df144/drivers/apple_embedded/os_apple_embedded.mm#L413-L420

I haven't tested this with older versions of Godot, but seeing as how this code hasn't changed in a while I have no reason to believe this is a recent regression.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP project.
2. Export MRP project to iOS.
3. Run the project on an iOS device.
4. Note the failure/error.

title: Focus events when embedding game in editor behaves differently on macOS
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.beta7

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7 - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

This is an off-shoot from #109633, which was partially addressed in #109724.

On Windows and Linux (X11) the focus events are emitted as follows:

- When in either mode, both application and window enter events are emitted when the user first hovers over the game viewport.
- When in either mode, both application and window exit events are emitted when the user clicks outside of the game viewport.
- When in floating mode, both application and window exit events are emitted when focus of the floating workspace window is lost.

However, on macOS, it now behaves as follows after #109724 was merged:

- When in either mode, only window focus events are emitted based on mouse input within the editor, and only when clicking, not when hovering[^1].
- When in floating mode, window exit events are emitted when focus of the floating workspace window is lost.
- When in non-floating mode, losing focus of the editor window will emit both application and window exit events.
- When in non-floating mode, gaining focus of the editor window will emit both application and window enter events.

It's unclear what the correct behavior should be in this case, and I would argue both have some weird quirks, but the fact that the behavior is different between different desktop platforms would seem to be somewhat problematic.

[^1]: `InputEventMouseMotion` is still sent to the application when unfocused though.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP on macOS.
2. Try both the floating and non-floating embedded mode.
3. Open the MRP on Windows or Linux/X11.
4. Try both the floating and non-floating embedded mode.
5. Note the difference in behavior.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[focus-events.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22082393/focus-events.zip)

title: Parameter "fd" is null when switching fonts in TextEdit
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5 beta 6 official, 4.5 beta 7 official
- Not Reproducible in:  4.4.1 official

### System information

MacOS Sequoia 15.6.1 (24G90),  Windows 10

### Issue description

If you 
* load a font dynamically 
* use it to override a theme font on a Text Edit
* do not keep a reference to it,
* remove the font override

Godot will generate a number of 'Parameter "fd" is null' errors proportional to the number of characters in the TextEdit.  If there are no characters in the TextEdit no errors are generated.
```
E 0:00:00:334   main.gd:23 @ swap_font(): Parameter "fd" is null.
  <C++ Source>  modules/text_server_adv/text_server_adv.cpp:2844 @ _font_get_ascent()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:23 @ swap_font()
                main.gd:12 @ _ready()
```
You can workaround this by keeping a reference to the font yourself.  This might be the intended behavior but it did not work this way in Godot 4.4.1 so I thought I'd report it.

### Steps to reproduce

Here's the source for the project below.
```gdscript
extends Node2D

@onready var text_edit = $TextEdit

var keep_reference = false
var _font_ref = null

func _ready():
	text_edit.text = "hello world"
	
	swap_font('custom')
	swap_font('default')
	

func swap_font(which):
	if(which == 'custom'):
		var dynamic_font = FontFile.new()
		dynamic_font.load_dynamic_font("res://CourierPrime-Regular.ttf")
		text_edit.add_theme_font_override('font', dynamic_font)
		if(keep_reference):
			_font_ref = dynamic_font
	else:
		text_edit.remove_theme_font_override('font')
```

If you open the project and run the main scene it will generate 44 errors.  

* You can change the text and rerun to see how the errors scale with characters in the TextEdit.
* If you change the text to an empty string then no errors are generated.
* If you set `keep_reference` to `true` and have text in the TextEdit then no errors are generated.  This is the workaround/what-you-should-actually-do mentioned above.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[fd_is_null.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22083421/fd_is_null.zip)
author: bitwes

title: Inspector: `Clear` option not visible when a `Resource`'s `@export`-ed property is a `Script`
body: ### Tested versions v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6607) - AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 w/ Radeon 890M (24 threads) ### Issue description When right-clicking a `Resource`'s property that has `@export`, if that property is set to a `Script`, the inspector does not show the `Clear` button to set the property to `null`. This is what I would expect to see: But when the property is set to a `Script`: As you can see, the `Clear` button is not shown on the `Script`. This happens regardless of the type of the property. Even when the property is typed to `Resource`, when setting it to a `Script` it still doesn't show `Clear`. Also, for some reason this issue does not apply when the property is exported from a `Node`. I'm guessing this is done to prevent people from clearing an object's script by clearing its `script` property, but this shouldn't apply to other properties. This issue is preventing me from adding a custom revert value for `Script` properties, since they cannot (easily) be set to `null` again afterwards. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a custom `Resource` script. 2. Add an `@export`-ed property of type `Script` (or an ancestor class). 3. Create an instance of the custom `Resource`. 4. Set the property to a `Script`. 5. Right-click the property, and observe that the `Clear` button is missing. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22083437/mrp.zip)

title: Borderless fullscreen with v-sync acts as if it crosses over to second monitor on the right
body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in: 4.5.dev1, 4.5.dev3, 4.5.beta4, 4.5.beta5, and 4.5.beta7 Not reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable ### System information Godot v4.5.beta7 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors, Primary 144 Hz and Secondary 60 Hz - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6800 (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.21025.1024) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) ### Issue description The vsync'd framerate of the project reflects the same behavior as crossing two monitors with mismatched refresh rates when it is in borderless fullscreen _only_ when the lower refresh rate monitor is on the right. When across two screens (in this case, one at 144 Hz and the other at 60 Hz) and the window's main screen is the highest framerate, the framerate becomes the largest multiple of the lower refresh rate below the higher refreshrate (in this case 120 FPS). This should not occur when in borderless fullscreen because it should be only display on one monitor. This video was captured using the provided project. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f1172b6f-c1d1-4668-a2ea-80efd4ed457f ### Steps to reproduce This may be difficult to reproduce without having the proper monitor setup. 1. (Assuming you are on Windows) Go into your display settings and rearrange your displays as such, where Display 1 has the higher refresh rate and Display 2 has the lower. - 2. Open and run the main scene. Ensure it's not embedded in the game view. 3. Press F11 to enter borderless fullscreen, F12 to enter exclusive fullscreen, and F10 to exit fullscreen. Windowed and exclusive fullscreen should behave as expected. 4. Rearrange your displays so that the left side of Display 2 is no longer touching the right side of Display 1. 5. Repeat steps 2 and 3. The problem in borderless fullscreen goes away. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [refresh rate test project 4.5.beta7.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22083597/refresh.rate.test.project.4.5.beta7.zip)
author: PsiGreenEx

title: StyleBoxTexture Texture Margin act like a MarginContainer
body: ### Tested versions 4.4.1 stable 4.5.Beta7 ### System information Godot v4.5.beta7 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8115) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9700K CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads) - 63.91 GiB memory ### Issue description after finally getting started on working with themes, i start to run into one issue after another. this one is what i understand the least. why is the TEXTURE margin of a background StyleBoxTexture acting like a MarginContainer placed between the node and it's sub nodes? it should be purely on the texture like for a NinePatchRect and ignore the children like the label for the button. the top one is a button and the bottom one is a NinePatchRect + Label. both have the same texture and regionRect for the margin and both are at their minimum height. i would expect that the theme behaves more like the NinePatchRect and maybe has a separate MarginContainer margin for the sub children that can be configured that doesn't affect the background texture. the thing that i find weird is that the Expand Margins of the StyleBoxContainer are purely texture based and don't affect anything else of the node. i would expect both of them to behave the same as it is texture settings and not node settings. btw, this also shifts the Center position of the Label to the side if the needed margins for the texture aren't symetrical for the texture. ### Steps to reproduce - make a control scene - add button - add a theme - give the button in the theme a StyleBoxTexture for the Normal style - set the texture and texture Margin like shown above - add a NinePatchRect - add the texture - set the same margins - add a Label as Child. - set the label text as Centered. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: Web Export Crashes on iOS iPad 17.x
body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in Godot 4.3, 4.4, 4.4.1, 4.5.beta7 ### System information Broken: iPad 9th Generation, iPadOS 17.2 ~~ Fixed: iPadOS 18.6.2 ### Issue description When using html export on the previous major version of iOS, after some time the whole page will crash and reload; this happens with every browser. There are no console logs or noticeable spikes in cpu/memory https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/25735704-26d0-48ed-9ca4-e3a2f36486bb On my primary project this happens in a matter of seconds. In this reproduction project it takes anywhere from 1-3 minutes to crash (and not consistent). Updating to the latest 18.x iOS fixed this. I suspect this may be a OS issue, but wanted to report as I'm sure others will run into this too ### Steps to reproduce - Create a new project - Add audio and set it to loop (I suspect audio isn't needed, it just helps it happen sooner than an empty project), I used "Cloud Dancer" from [incompetech](https://incompetech.com/music/royalty-free/music.html) for something similar in length and size to my project - Create a basic scene - Export to web - Play on mobile safari 17.x (upload somewhere, mock local https, etc), and wait for the crash, a retry may be required. I suspect more complicated projects will happen sooner ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [ios-bug-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22085174/ios-bug-mrp.zip)
author: JoelCreatesGames

title: Using web export, godot always sets the page title and favicon of the page it runs on.
body:
### Tested versions

Currently using 4.5-beta7, but this has been true for past versions as well.

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7 - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M1 - Apple M1 (8 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using the web export, when the game first starts, the title and favicon of the page we're running on changes to the name and icon of the project.

This is fine for exports where you only run your game, but when embedding a godot export in a larger project, this behaviour is undesirable.

In my opinion, the engine itself should not try to change title of the page unless explicitly told to, or at the very least this should be an option when creating the web export.

### Steps to reproduce

The easiest way to reproduce the problem is:

- Export the project using a custom html shell that has a custom title different from your project name.
- Run the game inside a browser.
- Notice that while loading, the page title will be the one from the html shell, but when the game loads, the title becomes your project name.

In the attached project, you just need to export and run the project in a browser. The title is "My Template" during loading and will become "HTML Title set by Godot" with the godot icon after loading.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[html-title-set-by-godot.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22094644/html-title-set-by-godot.zip)

title: Skinned meshes disappear on second and subsequent runs unless cache is cleared
body:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4.1 Stable and Godot 4.3 Stable 


### System information

Pixel8a - Android 14, Chrome-based preview - Godot 4.4.1 Stable 

### Issue description

On Pixel 8a, any mesh with a skeleton renders correctly on the first run of the game. On the second and all subsequent runs, the meshes with skeletons are no longer visible.
If browser cache/data is cleared, the meshes appear again, but only for the first run.

I have tried:

Disabling shader caching (no effect).

Reproducing with a simple scene (only a skinned mesh, skeleton, and animation) — the issue still occurs.

### Steps to reproduce

- Export a Godot 4.4.1 project with a skinned mesh (e.g., Character with Skeleton3D and animation).
- Open the exported HTML5 build in Chrome on Pixel 8a.
- Observe that on the first launch, the mesh is visible.
- Close the tab/browser and re-open the game.
- The skinned mesh is no longer visible.
- Clear browser cache → mesh reappears (but disappears again on the next launch).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[skinned-meshes-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22098382/skinned-meshes-bug.zip)

title: PRs closing unintended issues
body:
### Tested versions

NA

### System information

NA

### Issue description

I just wanted to bring this to attention as @Repiteo encountered the same thing I did here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/109846#issuecomment-3243437746

I've had instances where I put in a PR that is related to other open issues, but the PR itself should not close that issue.  It would be nice as a contributor to have the permissions to make modifications on PRs that I author in regards to what issues the PR should / shouldn't be closing.

### NOTE

I have no idea if this is possible.  I just wanted to bring it to godot dev's attention as I've had godot devs with higher permissions ask me to make sure issues aren't linked to my PR.  I'm pretty confident I, nor other contributors, presently have the ability to do this.

I'll have to track down the PR in particular I ran into this with.  It was a while back.  @Repiteo's comment reminded me of this with the latest PR that went through.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Put in a PR as a contributor
2. Mention an issue in the description
3. See that there is no way for a contributor to prevent that PR from closing the issue

I'm guessing this is a permissions issue where everyday contributors need the permission to link and unlink issues from PRs for when PRs should / shouldn't be closing issues.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

NA

title: `emulate_touch_from_mouse` doesn't work in sub-windows
body:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

Tested with editor setting `run/platforms/linuxbsd/prefer_wayland` enabled as well as when disabled.

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition) on Wayland - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility)

### Issue description

Non-embedded sub-windows, don't receive touch input events when `input_devices/pointing/emulate_touch_from_mouse` is set to `true` in the project settings.

### Steps to reproduce

1. In Project Settings:
   - Check `input_devices/pointing/emulate_touch_from_mouse`
   - Uncheck `display/window/subwindows/embed_subwindows`
1. Create a script that tests received input events for touch. For example:
    ```gdscript
    # test.gd
    extends Node
    func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	    if event is InputEventScreenTouch or event is InputEventMouseButton:
	        print(event.as_text())
    ```
1. Create an empty scene, add a `Window` Node, and attach the script.
1. When running the scene and clicking inside of the sub-window, you should see in the output log that only mouse events are received.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[touch_input_test_subwindow_godot-4-4-1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22099724/touch_input_test_subwindow_godot-4-4-1.zip) (1.6 kB)

title: Image.create_empty() always creates zero-sized images in Godot 4.3
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible: v4.3.stable

### System information

mac os ventura 13.7.7, godot v4.3.stable, Vulkan 1.2.283, Intel - Intel(R) Iris(TM) Plus Graphics 640

### Issue description

When calling `Image.create_empty()`, the created image always has size `(0,0)`.  
This happens even when providing valid width/height values.  

Expected behavior:  
`Image.create_empty(width, height, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)` should create an image with the given dimensions.  


### Steps to reproduce

Create a new project, attach this script to a Node, and run:

```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:
	option1()
	option2()
	option3()

func option1() -> void:
	var img := Image.new()
	img.create_empty(500, 500, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)
	print("Image size: ", img.get_size())
	print("h: ", img.get_height())
	print("w: ", img.get_width())
	
func option2() -> void:
	var img := Image.new()
	img.create_empty(500, 500, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)
	img.resize(500,500)
	print("Image size: ", img.get_size())
	print("h: ", img.get_height())
	print("w: ", img.get_width())
	
func option3() -> void:
	var img := Image.new()
	img.create(500, 500, false, Image.FORMAT_RGBA8)
	img.resize(500,500)
	print("Image size: ", img.get_size())
	print("h: ", img.get_height())
	print("w: ", img.get_width())


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22100442/mrp.zip)

title: Exporting raw variant fields in scripts have different results in the editor inspector depending on how it's written
body: ### Tested versions v4.5.beta7.official [4ebf67c12] ### System information Godot v4.5.beta7 - Windows 10 (build 19044) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL ES 3/ANGLE (Compatibility) - ANGLE (NVIDIA, NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (0x00001C82) Direct3D11 vs_5_0 ps_5_0, D3D11-31.0.15.4617) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-7700 CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads) - 15.93 GiB memory ### Issue description There are four different ways of writting the export for a raw variant field, and only one of them has the result a common user would expect when learning about the possibility of exporting raw variant fields: ```gdscript @export var m_with_type_without_default: Variant @export var m_with_type_with_default: Variant = 'm_with_type_with_default' @export var m_without_type_with_default = 'm_without_type_with_default' @export var m_without_type_without_default ``` Of these four usages, only the first one works, drawing a field that let you change the type ```gdscript @export var m_with_type_without_default: Variant ``` the next two exports become tied to the type of the initial value and do not let you change it. And lastly, the fourth type doesn't work at all, it raises a parser error with the message ```Error at (, ): Cannot use simple "@export" annotation with variable without type or initializer, since type can't be inferred.``` Even though I think most users whould expect all four syntaxes to be possible, the ones where a default value is provided are the most important, since it is pretty common for anyone to export a field with a default value, but none of the exports that have a default value allow changing the type. The exports with default value are being tied to the initial type but please note that I'm assigning the value with ```=```, not with ```:=```. We could even argue that makes sense for the value to the 'locked' when using ```:=``` but not when using ```=``` ### Steps to reproduce 1. create a new project in godot 4.5 beta 7 2. create a new scene 3. create a script in the root node 4. paste the code bellow in the script ```gdscript extends Node # this is the only one that allows changing the type in the inspector @export var m_with_type_without_default: Variant # this one does not allow changing the type in the inspector even though is typed as 'Variant' @export var m_with_type_with_default: Variant = 'm_with_type_with_default' # this one is untyped (and thus should be identical to typing as Variant) but also does not allow changing the type in the inspector @export var m_without_type_with_default = 'm_without_type_with_default' # this one could work but raises a parser error # @export var m_without_type_without_default # Error at (13, 1): Cannot use simple "@export" annotation with variable without type or initializer, since type can't be inferred. ``` Or just check the Main.tscn scene in the attached MRP ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [ExportVariantBug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22101500/ExportVariantBug.zip)
author: Gnumaru

title: Mobile: NaN is visible in specular reflections on smooth metallic materials (regression from FP16 optimizations)
body: - *Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/111894.* ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.5.beta1, 4.5.beta7 - Not reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.dev6 ### System information Godot v4.5.beta (80a219a58) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 575.64.05) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory ### Issue description Mobile | Forward+ -|- | Note that the issue above is with the ReflectionProbe visible, but it occurs without the ReflectionProbe too. The NaN is visible as long as you have a DirectionalLight with shadows enabled, and the reflection is seen in a shaded area. The issue also manifests in specular lobes on materials using per-vertex shading: Mobile | Mobile (llvmpipe, unaffected by the issue) -|- | I bisected the regression to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107119. cc @DarioSamo ### Steps to reproduce - Add a SphereMesh with StandardMaterial3D that has `roughness = 0.0` and `metallic = 1.0`. - Use a ProceduralSkyMaterial or PhysicalSkyMaterial with the default DirectionalLight3D properties, and enable shadows. - Move the sphere to a shaded area and look at the reflection. You may need to resize the viewport or change the camera FOV to make it more visible. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) - [test_specular_nan_mobile.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22102934/test_specular_nan_mobile.zip) - [test_lightmapgi_mobile.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22931963/test_lightmapgi_mobile.zip)

title: Method of an inner class doesn't get called when its connected to a signal from a different script
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6119) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600H with Radeon Graphics (12 threads)

### Issue description

When you connect a method from an inner class to a signal from a different script `Signal.connect` returns `OK` but the method is not getting called.

This got discovered because my instances of a custom `RefCounted` script were not getting freed after I spend a long time trying to get my callables working. And it appeared that if you put your method inside a lambda so that the signal connection would be working (see MRP for not working/working code), it will prevent the class instance from being freed.

If you comment out `bound_execute` connection in the inner class and leave only `local_execute` connection and remove (free) child node with the script, then `NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE` will never be recieved and the connection will still be intact.

For example add this code to the `my_root.gd` script
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	await get_tree().process_frame
	# Destroy child
	get_child(0).queue_free()
```
And make the inner class inside `my_child.gd` like this
```gdscript
class Test extends RefCounted:
	var _my_signal: Signal
	
	func _init(my_signal: Signal) -> void:
		_my_signal = my_signal
	
	func try_connect() -> void:
		var local_execute = func ():
			execute("from the local lambda")
		_my_signal.connect(local_execute)
	
	func execute(arg_to_be_bind: String) -> void:
		print("text: ", arg_to_be_bind)
	
	func _notification(what: int) -> void:
		if what == NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE:
			print("about to be freed")
```
and it will never be freed and the `local_execute` lambda will be getting called as long as the signal is emitting.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a script and make an inner class inside the script. Inside the inner class make a method that will be called by a signal
2. Pass a signal from another script inside the inner class
3. Connect the method to a signal inside the inner class or inside the script

For example (this is from MRP):
Script on scene's root node:
```gdscript
extends Control

signal tick()

func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
	tick.emit()
```
Script on a scene's child node:
```gdscript
extends Node

class Test extends RefCounted:
	var _my_signal: Signal
	
	func _init(my_signal: Signal) -> void:
		_my_signal = my_signal
	
	func try_connect() -> void:
		var bound_execute = execute.bind("from the bound execute")
		if _my_signal.connect(bound_execute) == OK:
			print("Signal is connected")
		
		# Uncomment to see it working
		#var local_execute = func ():
			#execute("from the local lambda")
		#_my_signal.connect(local_execute)
	
	func execute(arg_to_be_bind: String) -> void:
		print("text: ", arg_to_be_bind)

func _ready() -> void:
	var my_test = Test.new(get_parent().tick)
	my_test.try_connect()
```
The code will print
```
Signal is connected
```
and nothing else.
But if you uncomment the local lambda connection then both callables will be called
```
Signal is connected
text: from the bound execute
text: from the local lambda
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)


[signal-connection-to-inner-class.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22102937/signal-connection-to-inner-class.zip)

body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, ### Issue description The experimental GraphNode control, when instantiated, creates an HBoxContainer, with a Label inside, to display its title. When I run get_children() on the GraphNode, the HBoxContainer is omitted. I want to get that Label node for editing reasons (get its size or change its style or wrapping method). ### Steps to reproduce 1. In a blank scene add a GraphNode node 2. (Optional) add a child node to the GraphNode 3. Attach a script to the GraphNode 4. Call `get_children()` for the GraphNode from the script 5. Go to Remote Scene tree to observe the actual children nodes under GraphNode **Expected behavior**: The returned array should contain all children visible in the scene tree (HBoxContainer + added child node in step 2) **Actual behavior**: The HBoxContainer does not appear in the get_children() array ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) ``` extends GraphNode func _ready(): print(get_children()) pass ```

author: MadeScientist

title: Jolt physics server: support `min_height` and `max_height` as heightmap shape data to skip AABB calculation
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.dev7

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (97cf92a73) - Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon Graphics (radeonsi, renoir, ACO, DRM 3.63, 6.15.10-200.fc42.x86_64) - AMD Ryzen 9 4900HS with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 38.58 GiB memory

### Issue description

The Jolt physics server implementation does not yet support the `min_height` and `max_height` heightmap shape data, and calculates an AABB by iterating over the heights unconditionally:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/6c9aa4c7d3b9b91cd50714c40eeb234874df7075/modules/jolt_physics/shapes/jolt_height_map_shape_3d.cpp#L226

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/6c9aa4c7d3b9b91cd50714c40eeb234874df7075/modules/jolt_physics/shapes/jolt_height_map_shape_3d.cpp#L171-L193

It also does not yet return the minimum and maximum height:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/6c9aa4c7d3b9b91cd50714c40eeb234874df7075/modules/jolt_physics/shapes/jolt_height_map_shape_3d.cpp#L195-L201

It should support this `min_height` and `max_height` data like Godot Physics does, to skip the AABB calculation:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/6c9aa4c7d3b9b91cd50714c40eeb234874df7075/modules/godot_physics_3d/godot_shape_3d.cpp#L2213-L2237

### Steps to reproduce

Observe the keys `"min_height"` and `"max_height"` missing when running this script with the Jolt Physics server selected:

```gdscript
extends Node3D

var heightmap_shape_rid: RID

func _enter_tree() -> void:
	heightmap_shape_rid = PhysicsServer3D.heightmap_shape_create()
	PhysicsServer3D.shape_set_data(heightmap_shape_rid, {
		"width": 2,
		"depth": 2,
		"heights": PackedFloat32Array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0])
	})
	print(PhysicsServer3D.shape_get_data(heightmap_shape_rid))

func _exit_tree() -> void:
	PhysicsServer3D.free_rid(heightmap_shape_rid)
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[new_game_project_2025-09-03_16-18-39.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22120766/new_game_project_2025-09-03_16-18-39.zip)

title: Adding AnimationTree to a scene inheriting glb causes the AnimationLibrary to be saved in the scene
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in current master (6c9aa4c7d) and in 4.5-beta7
- Not reproducible in 4.4.1 stable
Using editor on Windows 10.

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (6c9aa4c7d) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated Radeon RX 580 Series (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 31.0.21921.1000) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads) - 31.90 GiB memory

### Issue description

When you create a scene inheriting from a glb with animations, there will be an AnimationPlayer in the scene, and its libraries have resource_path pointing to the glb. Good, you can save the scene and it's nice and slim. Add an AnimationTree and set the anim_player to the one from the glb. Now the tree can play the glb's animations, all good. But if you save the scene at this point, the library's storage moves from the glb into the scene inheriting it. Now the tscn file is bloated up with animations that don't inherit from the glb anymore.

In 4.4.1, the libraries property of AnimationTree is grayed out as soon as you set anim_player. Now in 4.5 it can be modified, which I presume has something to do with this behavior.

### Steps to reproduce

I created a MRP to show the issue fast:
1. Open asset_scene.tscn. This is just a scene inheriting from the included glb with no changes.
2. Observe the resource_path on the AnimationPlayer's libraries, it points to the glb.
3. Add an AnimationTree to the scene, and set the anim_player property.
4. Save the scene. Observe the resource_path on the AnimationPlayer's library resource.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[AnimLibraryMRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22127131/AnimLibraryMRP.zip)

title: Mouse button release signal sent to wrong Control when mouse dragged
body:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]



### System information

Windows 10 - Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.official.49a5bc7b6 - OpenGL API 3.3.0 NVIDIA 576.83 - Compatibility - Using Device: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU

### Issue description

When the mouse button is pressed on one Control, the mouse cursor dragged to a second Control and the mouse button released on that second Control, the mouse released signal is sent to the first Control instead of the second one.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a 2D Scene and add two TextureRect (named TextureRect1 and TextureRect2).
Add the icon.svg image as image to both TextureRect instances.
Move the first TextureRect to position (100, 100) (using Layout > Transform > Position)
Move the second TextureRect to position (300, 300) (using Layout > Transform > Position)
Attach a new script to TextureRect1 (named texture_rect.gd in my case)
In the 2D scene editor, select TextureRect1 and then the Node tab, and link the signals mouse_entered(), mouse_exited() and gui_input(...) to the created script.
Update the code of the methods as follows:

```gdscript
func _on_mouse_entered() -> void:
	print("Mouse entered " + name)


func _on_mouse_exited() -> void:
	print("Mouse entered " + name)


func _on_gui_input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event is InputEventMouseButton:
		if event.is_pressed():
			print("Mouse button pressed on " + name)
		elif not event.is_pressed():
			print("Mouse button released on " + name)
```

Go back to the 2D scene editor and attach the same script to TextureRect2.
Link the mouse_entered(), mouse_exited() and gui_input(...) signals to the script on TextureRect2 using the Node tab.

Run the script and select the current scene as the main scene.

Move the mouse over the TextureRect on the left (TextureRect1).
Press the mouse button on TextureRect1 and keep the mouse button down.
Move the mouse over the TextureRect on the right (TextureRect2) while keeping the mouse button down.
Release the mouse button over TextureRect2

The logs show that the mouse button was released over TextureRect1 instead of TextureRect2, even though the mouse_entered signal was processed correctly by TextureRect2.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[testmouseevents.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22132870/testmouseevents.zip)

title: Gradle generated Android dotnet exports are not 16kb page aligned
body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in Godot 4.5 Mono beta7 Linux build - Reproducible in Godot 4.5 Mono beta7 MacOs build ### System information Android 16 ### Issue description [Starting November 1st, 2025, all new apps and app updates that use native C/C++ code targeting Android 15+ devices submitted to Google Play must support 16 KB page sizes.](https://android-developers.googleblog.com/2025/07/transition-to-16-kb-page-sizes-android-apps-games-android-studio.html#:~:text=Starting%20November%201st%2C%202025%2C%20all,on%20the%20latest%20Android%20hardware.) The update was done for non-dotnet export templates and we assumed that'd be sufficient to also cover dotnet export templates. However by default dotnet Godot projects use `net8.0` but support for 16kb page size was only added to `net9.0`. As a result, gradle builds using the default `net8.0` end up with their dotnet native libraries not being 16kb page aligned. ### Steps to reproduce - Download Godot Mono 4.5 beta 7 - Create a new dotnet project, and attach a C# script - Download the export templates, and install the android build template - Create the Android export option and enable `Gradle build` - Export an Android build - Analyse the generated build using [Android Studio APK Analyser](https://developer.android.com/studio/debug/apk-analyzer) ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) New dotnet project
author: m4gr3d

title: Tiles with same y_sort origin and z_index aren't drawn at the same time
body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable.fedora.49a5bc7b6, 4.5-beta7 ### System information Fedora 42 - 4.4.1.stable.fedora.49a5bc7b6 - Forward+ and Compatibility - 16 × AMD Ryzen 7 PRO 7840U w/ Radeon 780M Graphics ### Issue description Tiles from one `TileMapLayer` are drawn on top of my character, but tiles from another `TileMapLayer` (with the exact same `y_sort` origin and `z_index`) are drawn below my character. I expect two tiles from two different `TileMapLayers` with the same `z_index` and `y_sort` origin to be drawn at the same time in relation to my character. I've double checked that everything has `y_sort` enabled and has the same `z_index`. ### Steps to reproduce Created a minimal player node and two TileMapLayers. Just walk into the walls and you'll see -- some walls are drawn on top of the player's head, but the rubble (from the same TileMapLayer) seems to be working. I've included two tiles from a different TileMapLayer off to the side -- same y_sort origin and z_index, but these working as expected. I've added a picture to point to the exact tiles I'm talking about. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22163663/test.zip)

title: mediump qualifier does not work on iOS
body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information iPhone 15 Pro Max iOS 17.1.1 (21B91) ### Issue description The shader included in the MRP specifies mediump, and I expect it to be computed with half on iOS. However, the generated MSL code looks like this: Just before conversion to MSL, I confirmed that the mediump specification is annotated with RelaxedPrecision in SPIR-V. However, in the generated MSL code, float is used instead of half. ### Steps to reproduce - Export an Xcode project for iOS. - Build and run it using Xcode, then check the shader code with GPU Frame Capture. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [shader-precision.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22165586/shader-precision.zip)
author: cpl-s-matsuyama

title: Expression ignores extra values at end
body:
### Tested versions

 - Reproducible in: Godot v4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon (TM) R9 200 Series (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 27.20.1034.6) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

If I create an Expression object, and then give it multiple separate values separated by spaces, it just ignores everything after the first space. This is illogical to me, as I would expect it to give a parse error since this would not be allowed in gdscript.

For example, "5  12983 ARGGG!! &%-_@@+++" is an utterly illogical expression which should result in an error, but instead it simply results in 5 and absolutely no errors.

I understand that this may be to help with users potentially entering bad input, but in my case I need to know they entered bad input (I'm making a programming game). I also know this would break compatibility if games already expect this, so I suggest that we just add a boolean option such as `ignore_extra_text` which defaults to `true`.

The work-around I would need to do to fix this in my game would be to _parse it myself_, which defeats the purpose of having the Expression class in the first place.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a script in a project.
Add the following:
```gdscript
var expr = Expression.new()
var err = expr.parse("5  12983 ARGGG!! &%-_@@+++")
print_debug(err) # OK

var result = expr.execute()
print_debug(result) # 5
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

author: tjohann

title: Spring Bone Collisions not colliding with newly created chains
body:
### Tested versions

Version 4.5 beta 6
Version 4.4.1

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

I've been writing tooling to edit skeleton hierarchies for adding spring bone chains.  I wrote a script to autogenerate settings / bone chains based on naming conventions.  This however disables the collisions with existing spring bone collisions, I can get around this if I reparent those collisions under the spring bone simulation.  This also happens when manually adding chains.

You can reopen the scene to also fix the issue

### Steps to reproduce

- Add a spring bone collision to a spring bone simulation
- Add a chain to the settings that would be effected by the spring bone collision
- Adjust the spring bone collision to see if it effects the chain (it does not)
- Fix A: Save and reopen the scene, collisions will be fixed
- Fix B: Cut and paste the spring bone collision back into the simulation (it should now effect the chains)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mvp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22172300/mvp.zip)
Add a chain for Lower Shirt 0_0 - Lower Shirt 0_2

title: MeshDataTool can crash editor when using precision=double
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.beta.mono.double.custom_build [4ed8c45ab] (master)

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta.mono.double (4ed8c45ab) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 31.11 GiB memory

### Issue description

When loading in certain meshes on a build using `precision=double`, MeshDataTool will crash when trying to access the array of tangents. 

I was able to fix by changing `real_t` to `float` when reading tangents in the MeshDataTool but considering the rendering server supports `PACKED_FLOAT64_ARRAY` for tangents, I'm concerned this is not a valid general solution.

### Steps to reproduce

Use `MeshDataTool.create_from_surface` in the attached MRP.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

title: Menu shortcuts do not work in EditorPlugin generated windows.
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1 official, 4.5 beta7 official

### System information

MacOS 15.6.1 (24G90)

### Issue description

Godot menus do not work when a window created by a plugin has focus.  On Mac, you can see the menu item flash, as if the shortcut was doing something, but the associated menu item is not triggered.  I thought this only applied to `add_tool_submenu_item` menu item shortcuts, but it appears to be almost all shortcuts.

I found this when implementing a pop-out feature for a bottom panel control.  I am essentially doing the same thing as the code below.  Once the control was popped out into the window, I noticed shortcuts were not working when the window had focus.

Is there a more formal way to create an "editor window", or to make a panel that pops out?  

### Steps to reproduce

* Run the MRP.
* Put the focus on the editor.
* Press `ctrl+0` (zero) and it will print a message.
* Focus on the window the plugin opened.
* Press `ctrl+0` and nothing happens.  On mac you can see the "Project" menu item flash.

You can also try other menu items, most don't work while the window has focus.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Here's the only code in the project.  
* It makes menu item in Project->Tools-Demo Menu->Test Menu.  
* The menu item has `ctrl + 0` shortcut.
* A message is printed when the menu is clicked or the shortcut triggers the menu.
* It creates a window and adds it to itself.

```gdscript
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

var sub_menu : PopupMenu = null
var window : Window = null


func _enter_tree() -> void:
	sub_menu = PopupMenu.new()
	add_tool_submenu_item("Demo Menu", sub_menu)
	sub_menu.index_pressed.connect(_on_sub_menu_index_pressed)

	sub_menu.add_item("Test Menu", 0)
	sub_menu.set_item_accelerator(0, KEY_MASK_CTRL | KEY_0)

	window = Window.new()
	window.size = Vector2(500, 500)
	window.title = "Shortcuts Don't Work Here"
	add_child(window)


func _exit_tree() -> void:
	sub_menu.free()
	window.free()


func _on_sub_menu_index_pressed(index):
	print("menu index ", index, " triggged")
```

[EditorShortcutDemo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22177626/EditorShortcutDemo.zip)

title: Godot Does Not Open Any Projects and Crashes With Signal 11
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in Godot Mono 4.4.1.

### System information

Windows 11 Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.mono.official.49a5bc7b6 - https://godotengine.org Vulkan 1.2.187 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: Intel - Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics

When opening any projects, the editor does not open. When opening from the terminal, this error appears:

title: Invalid or empty glyph font texture causes mipmap check to segfault
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible on: v4.4.1.stable.mono.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.rc.mono.custom_build [3c7f9b937](directly cloned from master branch)


### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Artix Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon, 01 Sep 2025 16:44:13 +0000 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 580 2048SP (RADV POLARIS10) - Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E3-1220 v3 @ 3.10GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

When working on my game, I have noticed that under quite flacky conditions, Godot would segfault when I added a button onto the scene.
After some investigation, I hooked Godot to GDB and came across this backtrace on crash:
```
0x000055555defa7e2 in Image::has_mipmaps (this=0x0) at core/io/image.cpp:459
459             return mipmaps;
(gdb) backtrace
#0  0x000055555defa7e2 in Image::has_mipmaps (this=0x0) at core/io/image.cpp:459
#1  0x0000555558c2b3d2 in TextServerAdvanced::_font_draw_glyph (this=0x5555636a4670, p_font_rid=..., p_canvas=..., p_size=14, p_pos=..., 
    p_index=52, p_color=..., p_oversampling=0) at modules/text_server_adv/text_server_adv.cpp:4004
#2  0x0000555558c4cbd9 in TextServerAdvanced::font_draw_glyph (this=0x5555636a4670, arg1=..., arg2=..., arg3=14, arg4=..., arg5=52, 
    arg6=..., arg7=0) at modules/text_server_adv/text_server_adv.h:930
#3  0x000055555d3042b7 in TextServer::shaped_text_draw (this=0x5555636a4670, p_shaped=..., p_canvas=..., p_pos=..., p_clip_l=0, 
    p_clip_r=118, p_color=..., p_oversampling=0) at servers/text_server.cpp:1797
#4  0x000055555d97b6ed in TextServerExtension::shaped_text_draw (this=0x5555636a4670, p_shaped=..., p_canvas=..., p_pos=..., p_clip_l=0, 
    p_clip_r=118, p_color=..., p_oversampling=0) at servers/text/text_server_extension.cpp:1570
#5  0x000055555cce783c in TextParagraph::draw (this=0x55556727de20, p_canvas=..., p_pos=..., p_color=..., p_dc_color=..., 
    p_oversampling=0) at scene/resources/text_paragraph.cpp:929

TextParagraph::draw -> TextServerExtension::shaped_text_draw -> TextServerAdvanced::font_draw_glyph -> TextServerAdvanced::_font_draw_glyph -> Image::has_mipmaps
```

Checking the offending code, it seems to code from [`modules/text_server_adv/text_server_adv.cpp`, around line 3999](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/3c7f9b937214068bc892be0d2bd9a8a7026edae1/modules/text_server_adv/text_server_adv.cpp#L3999)

The `Ref<Image> img = tex.image;` references the image from `ShelfPackTexture &tex = ffsd->textures.write[fgl.texture_idx];`. For some reason, seems that `fgl.texture_idx` inside the `ffsd->textures.write` array is null.
Adding `ERR_FAIL_NULL(img);` at the line after the `Ref<Image> img = tex.image;` seems to mitigate the issue, yet console now prints errors on the console about a null reference.

There doesn't seem to be any sort of visual bug with this patch?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the reproducible project mentioned at the MRP
2. Run it
3. Click on the only button
4. Click once on the dialogue to advance it once

I'm not sure if the code being in C# has any sort of relation to it, or if GDScript would achieve the same behavior
Going on step-by-step with the debugger seems to get into a `DialogueService.Generated.cs` before the segfault takes place, could be related?

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Reproducible project is available at: https://github.com/akatsukilevi/godot-bugreport-font-segfault

title: Issue: Android Export Fails with JDK Path Error in Steam Version on Arch Linux
body:
### Tested versions

Tested versions

    Version with the issue: Godot Steam Version (4.4.1.stable)

    Version without the issue: Godot from Arch Linux official repositories (4.4.1) - Works correctly ✅

    Additional testing: I have not tested other Godot versions (such as 4.3.stable or earlier 4.4 dev snapshots) due to limited time and resources. The focus of this report is to highlight the discrepancy between the Steam distribution and the official repository version.

    Conclusion: This bug appears to be specific to the Steam distribution/build of Godot 4.4.1, as the same official version from Arch repositories functions correctly with identical settings and projects.

### System information

System information      OS: Arch Linux x86_64 (Kernel: 6.16.4-arch1-1)      Desktop Environment: KDE Plasma 6.4.4 (Wayland)      CPU: Intel Pentium G3260 (2 cores @ 3.30GHz)      GPU: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 750 Ti (Discrete) - Driver version needed*      Memory: 12GB RAM (5.76GB used)      Storage: Ext4 filesystem      Network: Wired connection (enp3s0)      Shell: Bash 5.3.3

### Issue description

## Description of the Problem 
1. When I try to export my project for Android, the process seems to complete successfully ("everything is OK"). However, the editor also displays an error message: "Invalid Java SDK path in Editor Settings. Missing 'bin' directory".I then installed Godot from the Arch Linux official repositories using the command ** sudo pacman -S godot ** . Using the exact same export settings, the version from the official repositories works without any errors.
2. The key store path selected through the file manager will not be updated in the export settings.
   
 
## [The complete error report GitHub repository link](https://github.com/xuezhaju/Issue-Android-Export-Fails-with-JDK-Path-Error-in-Steam-Version-on-Arch-Linux)

### Steps to reproduce

### **Exact Steps to Reproduce**
1. Set up a clean installation of Arch Linux.
2. Install Steam via the command : 
    sudo pacman -S steam
3. Install the Godot Engine from the Steam client.
4. Clone the repository that demonstrates the issue:
    git clone https://github.com/xuezhaju/Qixi-Gift.git
5. Observe that the export process completes but the following error message is displayed:
6. Then you can see the error message:
    "Invalid Java SDK path in Editor Settings. Missing 'bin' directory"

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

### **Minimal reproduction project**

**N/A** - This issue is not project-dependent. The bug occurs universally in the Steam version of Godot when attempting Android export, regardless of project content. The error is related to JDK path detection in the editor itself.

However, for testing consistency, contributors can use any Godot project or create a new empty project to reproduce the issue.

- [The complete error report GitHub repository link](https://github.com/xuezhaju/Issue-Android-Export-Fails-with-JDK-Path-Error-in-Steam-Version-on-Arch-Linux)

title: Inspector array: expanded state follows original index after drag-reorder (should follow the moved element)
body:
### Tested versions


- Reproductible in : Godot v4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.5585) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

When reordering array elements in the **Inspector** by drag-and-drop **while one element is expanded**, the expanded/folded state stays at the **original index**, instead of following the element that was moved.

Example (1-based indices for clarity):

Before:

```
1: A
2: B (expanded)
3: C
```

Drag B above A ⇒ After:

```
1: B
2: A (expanded)  ← incorrect; the expansion remained at the old index
3: C
```

**Expected:**

```
1: B (expanded)
2: A
3: C
```

### Steps to reproduce

`res://my_item.gd`

   ```gdscript
   extends Resource
   class_name MyItem
   @export var label: String = ""
   ```

   `res://bug_demo.gd`

   ```gdscript
   extends Node
   @export var items: Array[MyItem]
   ```
2. Create a scene, attach `bug_demo.gd` to the root node.

3. In the Inspector, for `items`, click **Add Element** three times.

4. Expand each element and set `label` to **A**, **B**, and **C** respectively, so the array shows:

   ```
   0: MyItem (label="A")
   1: MyItem (label="B")
   2: MyItem (label="C")
   ```

5. **Expand** the second element (label **B**).

6. **Drag** the B element above A to reorder to **B, A, C**.

7. Observe that the **expanded** foldout is now on **A** (the element at the original index), not on **B** which was moved.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bugdemo.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22196611/bugdemo.zip)

title: Making Scene import default a file with a Skeleton3D makes project unable to open
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 (nvidia; 575.64.03) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600G with Radeon Graphics (12 threads)

### Issue description

When setting the Import default option for a scene, if the GLB or other scene file has a bone map included it will include this as part of the default option, and then when reloading the project it will be unable to open and return to the project manager, giving the following errors:
```
ERROR: Cannot get class 'BoneMap'.
   at: _instantiate_internal (core/object/class_db.cpp:549)
ERROR: Error parsing '/home/paperzlel/godot-projects/bone-map-crash-mrp/project.godot' at line 26: Can't instantiate Object() of type 'BoneMap' File might be corrupted.
   at: _load_settings_text (core/config/project_settings.cpp:824)
```
I would expect BoneMap data to not be included here, as it's something that varies depending on the model and depending on the target skeleton one is dealing with.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP
2. Right-click the `player_rigged.glb` and select "Advanced Import Options"
3. Select the Skeleton3D node in the hierarchy
4. Select `Retarget -> Bone Map` and create a new BoneMap
5. Inside the BoneMap, select a new SkeletonProfileHumanoid as the default profile
6. Click Reimport
7. With the file still selected, go to the Import tab (you may have to deselect and reselect it)
8. Click the Preset option, and hit "Set as Default for Scene"
9. Click Reimport
10. Reload the project by clicking `Project -> Reload Current Project`
11. The Project Manager should now appear, and any attempts to reload the project will return back to the project manager.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bone-map-crash-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22197153/bone-map-crash-mrp.zip)

Like that: ``` # parent_container.gd class_name ParentContainer extends RefCounted class Parent1: var property1 = 0 class SiblingClass1: var property2 = 0 ``` ``` # main.gd @tool extends EditorScript func _run() -> void: # when typing `ChildClass.` it adds all Siblings inner classes including itself for suggestion. ChildClass.SiblingClass1 # this gives a parse error as intended although it was suggested # but it is more confusing with instances. var child_object: ChildClass = ChildClass.new() child_object.SiblingClass1 # the error only appear during runtime interruption although it was suggested by the autocomplete feature. ``` ### Steps to reproduce Here siblings' inner classes of the parent class are suggested what happens: Even the parent itself: Also, this is something, but I believe it is better to be in a separate issue or feature proposal: As autocompletion of inner classes is not supported on inheritance `extends`! ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) Here where I tested this behavior with multiple elements: [InnerClassInheritance.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22198017/InnerClassInheritance.zip) It seems that this is working as expected with parent's siblings that are attributes, methods, and signals. But not the case with siblings' inner classes.

In 4.5, If I try to make text appear in a RichTextLabel which has horizontal justification set to RIGHT, and i move the Visible Ratio while text is set to "Characters Before Shaping", it still makes the text appear from the left.  This was not the case in 4.4, which had text appear from the right even as I was growing it with Visible Ratio. 

For the record, only "Characters Before Shaping" achieved the desired effect in 4.4, and trying other modes in both 4.4 and 4.5 did not replicate its functionality.

title: Scons for iOS fails when generate_bundle=yes and MoltenVK already exists at target location
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.beta

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta.mono (474735fd3) - macOS Sequoia (15.3.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M4 (Apple9) - Apple M4 (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

I'm compiling Godot for iOS. I've already compiled and exported successfully before. 

The `generate_bundle=yes` errors out if MoltenVK is already at expected location.

This command fails:
```
scons platform=ios target=template_debug module_mono_enabled=yes generate_bundle=yes -j6 || exit 1
```

build output:
```
scons: Reading SConscript files ...
Building for platform "ios", architecture "arm64", target "template_debug".
scons: done reading SConscript files.
scons: Building targets ...
[ 83%] Generating platform/ios/generate_bundle ...
[ 99%]  Copying "ios" debug framework
MoltenVK found at: /Users/Joel/VulkanSDK/1.4.321.0/macOS/lib/MoltenVK.xcframework
scons: *** [platform/ios/generate_bundle] /Users/Joel/Documents/src/storiator-godot/godot/bin/ios_xcode/MoltenVK.xcframework: File exists
scons: building terminated because of errors.
```

Without generate_bundle it works as expected with no errors, but it forces me to manually do the generate_bundle steps.
```
scons platform=ios target=template_debug module_mono_enabled=yes generate_bundle=yes -j6 || exit 1
```


The offending code in platform_methods.py:
```
    if use_mkv:
        mvk_path = detect_mvk(env, "ios-arm64")
        if mvk_path != "":
            shutil.copytree(mvk_path, app_dir + "/MoltenVK.xcframework")
```
it has no checks for whether it exists already

we have a couple options to fix:
1. conditionally skip copying
2. delete it if it exists and replace it
3. use `dirs_exist_okay=True` to do partial override

I think 1 is probably the right fix? happy to push a fix regardless if others agree it should be fixed.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Compile for iOS with generate_bundle=yes enabled.
2. Compile again, with generate_bundle=yes enabled. It will fail.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Sorry, don't have this right now.

I can fix the issue myself if people agree it should be fixed.

title: Expression class cannot handle operator `is` and `as` properly
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.rc1.official [08e9e6b29]

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.4.1.stable - Compatibility - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (amd, 32.0.21025) - Intel Core i5-12600KF CPU

### Issue description

It seems that `Expression.execute` handles expressions this way:

- Operator `is` always evals to its left operand, instead of a boolean.
- Operator `as` always evals to its left operand, instead of a type cast result.
- Unable to identify non-exist classes appearing as right operand when the 2 operators are involved

### Steps to reproduce

Operator `is` evals to its left operand:

```gdscript
var expr := Expression.new()
expr.parse("obj is Node", ["obj"])
print(expr.execute([Node.new()])) # "<Node#30870078914>"
```

Operator `as` evals to its left operand:

```gdscript
var expr := Expression.new()
expr.parse("obj as CSGBox3D", ["obj"])
print(expr.execute([RefCounted.new()])) # "<RefCounted#-9223372005984696941>"
```

Unable to identify non-exist classes appearing as right operand:

```gdscript
var expr := Expression.new()
expr.parse("true if obj is Abracadabra else false", ["obj"]) # OK, but no such class
print(expr.execute([Node.new()])) # "true"
print(expr.has_execute_failed()) # "false"
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

File: [ExpressionIssueMrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22203674/ExpressionIssueMrp.zip)

Run: Just run the project and see console.
