title: EditorInterface.get_script_editor().editor_script_changed emits with corrupt script reference
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.1 official, 4.5 rc1 official, probably many earlier versions

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc1 - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Pro (Apple7) - Apple M1 Pro (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

In very specific and obscure scenarios the script reference that `EditorInterface.get_script_editor().editor_script_changed` sends will have a `null` base script (when it shouldn't) and calling `get_method_list()` on the script will return an empty array (when it shouldn't).

This has been happening in https://github.com/bitwes/Gut for awhile but I've never been able reproduce it reliably.  I'm now able to detect when this occurs and print a warning for users.  This warning will appear in the GUT release for Godot 4.5 and may end up providing more information from users if this proves hard to determine the cause.

https://github.com/bitwes/Gut/issues/754

### Steps to reproduce

I have tried to replicate the issue in a simpler project but have not had any luck.  I've only seen this when opening scripts that inherit from `GutTest`.  This might be because I'm not really looking for it in other scripts.  I'm not sure if there is something about `GutTest` that is breaking things, or if this will happen with other scripts with multiple levels of inheritance.


1. Clone GUT `godot_4_5` branch:  
```
git clone https://github.com/bitwes/Gut -b godot_4_5
```
2. Open the GUT project in Godot in Godot 4.5 rc1 (or later)
3. Open script `res://test/unit/test_test.gd`
4. Launch VSCode for the root of the project (with the Godot plugin).
5. Ensure it is connected.
6. Quit Godot
7. Launch Godot (before VSCode quits trying to reconnect)
8. Open `res://test/unit/test_gut.gd`
9. Warning generated by GUT appears.

This is just the shortest list of steps.  After the warning appears once, it will appear for many of the test scripts (scripts that inherit from `GutTest`) that are opened.  This might be an LSP issue, so other editors that establish a connection might cause it as well.



### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

```
git clone https://github.com/bitwes/Gut -b godot_4_5
```

title: Microphone fails on Android after app reopen or repeated recordings in Godot 4.4.1 / 4.5-rc1
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:

Godot 4.4.1 (official) or Godot 4.5 rc1

### System information

Windows 10 (Editor) , Android 12 (Release)

### Issue description

I'm developing a chat app and the voice input works not good, my mobile device based on Android 12.

Behaviour:
Fresh install on device: recording works perfectly, both on first launch and during the same session.
After quitting and restarting the app: first recording still succeeds, but all subsequent attempts capture only noise—no valid audio data.

Additional observations:
The official "Audio Mic Record Demo" fails on first launch on Android, yet works after reopen it.
And I have another version when debugging, that show the opposite pattern: it record correctly on first install, but break after a restart.
So the version attached is currently the "least broken" I have.

Voice input is a critical feature for my app, so I'm happy to run any tests or provide further logs if needed.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Export the project to Android
2. Install and run for first time, maybe nothing wrong
3. Kill the app and reopen it, the first time recording maybe good
4. Try again, and nothing could be recorded

title: Can't override `_to_string` of `InputEventAction`
body:
### Tested versions

4.4.1, 4.5

### System information

Linux

### Issue description

Can't override `_to_string` of `InputEventAction` because the `to_string` is already override in Godot source code:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2dd26a027a99633231184616d4dd287bbdd1c0a3/core/input/input_event.h#L496
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2dd26a027a99633231184616d4dd287bbdd1c0a3/core/input/input_event.cpp#L1657-L1660

This issue exists in both gdscript and gdextension.

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
extends Node2D

class InputEventA extends InputEventAction:
	func _to_string() -> String:
		return "A"

func _ready() -> void:
	print(self.to_string())
	var a := InputEventA.new()
	print(a.to_string())

func _to_string() -> String:
	return "This Node"

```
This prints:
```
This Node
InputEventAction: action="", pressed=false

```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: AnimationTree continuously sets property values even when no animation is playing
body: ### Tested versions v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M1 - Apple M1 (8 threads) ### Issue description The AnimationTree appears to trigger continuous property assignments, even when no animation is playing. This results in the property being set every frame, which can potentially cause performance issues. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a Node2D with an exported float property that has a custom setter printing to the console whenever it is assigned. 2. Add an AnimationPlayer with a simple animation that modifies this float once (no loop). 3. Create an AnimationTree, assign it an AnimationNodeBlendTree as the root, and add a single AnimationNodeAnimation that references the animation from step 2. Connect this to the output. (The AnimationNode setup can vary. It is the same result with different AnimationNode setups, e.g. an AnimationNodeStateMachine.) title: OBJ with MTL import fails to import normal map
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5-rc1

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc1 - Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon Graphics (radeonsi, renoir, ACO, DRM 3.63, 6.15.10-200.fc42.x86_64) - AMD Ryzen 9 4900HS with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 38.58 GiB memory

### Issue description

Godot does not import normal maps specified in `.mtl` files like this:

```
map_Bump -bm 1.000000 Textures/T_Plaster_Normal.png
```

Apparently such an MTL file was created by Blender 4.0.2 (it comes from a Quaternius asset pack), so I think this format should be supported.

We can see in the source code why it does not work:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/2dd26a027a99633231184616d4dd287bbdd1c0a3/editor/import/3d/resource_importer_obj.cpp#L177-L184

Firstly it does not consider `map_Bump` (which may be nonstandard, but evidently is used).

Secondly, it does not consider the possibility of texture options such as `-bm 1.000000` appearing before/after the path.

In this context a quote from [the Wikipedia article on the OBJ format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wavefront_.obj_file#Vendor-specific_alterations) is relevant:

> According to the spec, options are supposed to precede the texture filename.
> However, at least one vendor generates files with options at the end. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the MRP
2. Notice the missing normals (the bricks should stick out from the wall).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[godot_obj_wall_bump_2025-09-09_11-31-47.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22230975/godot_obj_wall_bump_2025-09-09_11-31-47.zip)

title: `get_global_mouse_position()` does not work outside of window in wayland
body:
### Tested versions

v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - zink Vulkan 1.4(NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVK GA102) (MESA_NVK)) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600X 6-Core Processor (12 threads)

### Issue description

`Node2D.get_global_mouse_position()` doesnt return the mouse position outside of the window while using wayland, whereas when the configuration `run/platforms/linuxbsd/prefer_wayland` is disabled the coordinate update reliably outside of window can return negative value, even when unfocused. wayland also slower in updating the value.

i assume this is related to the wayland promised security to restrict global access to mouse pointer event.

without this making an eye app (like the classic _xeyes_) that can look at mouse position would be more difficult if not impossible to implement.

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
extends Node2D

func _process(delta: float) -> void:
	var position: Vector2 = get_global_mouse_position()
	print(position)
```

title: `SkeletonModifier3D._process_modification_with_delta` is called with a `delta` value of `0.0`
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.rc1 [08e9e6b29]
- Not Reproducible in v4.5.beta6 [d5ad0556a]

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.5.rc1 - Forward+

### Issue description

If a node inheriting SkeletonModifer3D is present in the main scene when the application starts, the `_process_modification_with_delta` method is initially called with a `delta` value of `0.0`, which caused some downstream NaN issues. This seems like a bug to me because it seems unexpected that the frame delta should ever be `0.0`, and thus requires always checking for this case. The latest docs do not warn about this issue either.

(Totally separate issue, but it was even harder to diagnose because the debugger is displaying NaN values as `0.0`).

### Steps to reproduce

I have included a MRP, but it is trivial to reproduce with out it.

1. Create a new scene with a Skeleton3D as root. No bones or any other data is required.
2. Create the following class:
```
class_name ZeroDelta extends SkeletonModifier3D

func _process_modification_with_delta(delta):
    if delta == 0.0:
        breakpoint
```
3. Add an instance of ZeroDelta as a child of the Skeleton3D.
4. Run the scene.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[skeleton-modifier-3d-zero-delta.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22235910/skeleton-modifier-3d-zero-delta.zip)

title: Error when changing texture while building compute list
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.beta7.mono.official [4ebf67c12]

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta7.mono - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 7 1700 Eight-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.95 GiB memory

### Issue description

Changing a curve of a curve texture in the inspector, while a compute list happens to be in the progress of being built, causes the error:
```
E 0:00:09:128   test_compute.gd:8 @ _process(): Updating textures is forbidden during creation of a draw or compute list
  <C++ Error>   Condition "draw_list.active || compute_list.active" is true. Returning: ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER
  <C++ Source>  servers/rendering/rendering_device.cpp:1571 @ texture_update()
  <Stack Trace> test_compute.gd:8 @ _process()
```

### Steps to reproduce

A node with the following code will simulate a game that runs compute every frame:
```gdscript
func _process(delta: float) -> void:
	var rd := RenderingServer.get_rendering_device()
	var compute_list := rd.compute_list_begin()
	for i in 1000000: ## mimic a long command list
		pass
	rd.compute_list_end()
```
If you run the game in the editor, and change a curve texture on a Sprite2D (maybe other textures too, but I keep this narrow), chances are that it will try to update the curve texture after rd.compute_list_begin() and before rd.compute_list_end(). That will produce an error, and fail to update the texture.

Uneducated suggestions:
- Only update textures between frames.
- Or maybe check if there currently is an active compute or draw list, and have a more graceful response than just throwing an error and failing to update the texture.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp-change-texture-during-compute.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22240579/mrp-change-texture-during-compute.zip)

title: C#: `Color` default assignement is not the same as in GDScript
body:
### Tested versions

4.5.rc1.mono, 4.4.stable.mono

### System information

### Issue description

When creating a `Color` property and leaving it with the default assignment in C#, its values will default to (0, 0, 0, 0). This is not the same behavior as in GDScript, which will default to (0, 0, 0, 1).

```cs
[Export] public Color ColorCSharpBugged; // Will create a new Color with these RGB value: (0, 0, 0, 0), not the same as in GDScript
[Export] public Color ColorCSharpNotBugged = new (0,0,0); // Will create a new Color with these RGB value: (0, 0, 0, 1), same as in GDScript
```

### Steps to reproduce

Create a new C# Script
Put a property of type `Color` and leave it with the default assignment
Print it out or export it

title: SpotLight3D culling happens too early for with angles above 90 degrees
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.3.stable, 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.rc1

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (6c9aa4c7d) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 575.64.05) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

SpotLight3D culling happens too early for with ultrawide angles (above 90 degrees). The higher the angle, the more likely the issue will appear when the camera moves or rotates. The issue also depends on the viewport's aspect ratio, with narrower aspect ratios more likely to encounter the issue.

This issue affects all rendering methods.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b3d18a1d-7a1a-4e0b-a1fa-84dede746efd

Note that on Forward+, this is **not** a clustering issue anymore, since it was fixed by https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100236. The issue is likely to be on the CPU culling side instead.

PS: I've also noticed that in Compatibility, the Camera3D preview in the inspector will have incorrect lighting (it looks like it uses the editor viewport lights' positions, but the actual camera for mesh rendering). Not sure if this is related, but I couldn't reproduce this in a blank project.

### Steps to reproduce

- Add a SpotLight3D node. Set its angle to a value like 175 degrees.
- Move and rotate the camera in the 3D editor. The light will eventually disappear, even though it should logically still be on screen.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test_spotlight_culling.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22244377/test_spotlight_culling.zip)

title: _draw() is called an additional time per frame when queue_redraw() is connected to Container's sort_children or pre_sort_children signals
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable.mono


### System information

Windows 11 - 4.4.1.stable.mono - Compatibility

### Issue description

_draw() is supposed to be called once per frame, after queue_redraw() has been called, either manually or by the engine.

however, if queue_redraw() is connected to a Container's sort_children or pre_sort_children signals, or both, then _draw() is called an additional time per frame.


### Steps to reproduce

1. using a node that extends Container, connect sort_children or pre_sort_children or both to queue_draw()
2. override _draw() to print how many frames have been drawn `Engine.get_frames_drawn()`
3. resize the node to emit the pre_sort_children and sort_children signals
4. notice that _draw() is called an additional time per frame

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

```gdscript
@tool
extends Container

# open container.tscn
# resize the node to emit sort_children signal
# in the output, you should notice that _draw is called twice in a single frame

func _ready() -> void:
	# _draw() is called an additional time when 
	# pre_sort_children, sort_childre, or both, are connected to queue_redraw
	
	#pre_sort_children.connect(queue_redraw)
	sort_children.connect(queue_redraw)

func _draw() -> void:
	print("frames(%s) / ticks(%s)" % [Engine.get_frames_drawn(), Time.get_ticks_usec()])
```

[draw-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22252055/draw-mrp.zip)

title: Moving some assets is not working as expected (bonemaps?)
body: ### Tested versions - Reproductible in: Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.official.49a5bc7b6 ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) ### Issue description Hello, A few days ago I've started creating a third person shooter controller for Godot 4. At some point, I wanted to move the files into the "addons" directory. At first, I moved everything at once, and it failed, some files were corrupt. So I did it manually,, file per file, in the commit history https://github.com/selgesel/godot4-third-person-controller/compare/main...solodeveloping:godot4-third-person-shooter:main you can look at Sep 1, 2025 ("[addons: moving player directory to addons and creating the addon](https://github.com/selgesel/godot4-third-person-controller/commit/770267c5167e422bd3225bda4a4655b9574f47df)") for instance), there is a bunch of commits made for this. I thought it had worked and stopped thinking about it. Yesterday, I wanted to use the controller in another project, and it did not worked, some files were corrupt when copying the addon's content to the other project. When copying the files from one project to another, there are only yellow errors, and then when starting the game, the referenes are broken. The warnings were quite unclear, I had the presence of mind of cloning the project in another directory. There, I had a red error "file not found". So I fixed it by editing the file manually in a text editor, and it worked, in the 2nd clone and in the other project. What it looks like now: "PATH:Armature/Skeleton3D": { "retarget/bone_map": Resource("res://addons/ThirdPersonShooter/assets/Mixamo BoneMap.tres") } And the commit: https://github.com/selgesel/godot4-third-person-controller/commit/ea3fea75f149674e76bd1b981e291fe568bbef8c What I think is happening, since I could work on the project even though the reference was broken, is that there is a UID in the cache, which allowed me to work on it, but when moving the content to another project, the cache is not present, so it fails. Note that I was lucky to have a red error, because the original fork owner had commit the .godot/ folder to Git, and I could have done that. Issues: - Moving some assets (bonemaps?) fails - When doing it slowly (?) it works but some references/UIDs are broken - Godot, when importing the folders in another project does not provide a clear error Error when opening the clone project: Error when opening the file: Error after reloading the project: What the scene look like normally: ### Steps to reproduce I guess you can fork on my last commit without the addons directory and move the content of the project in an addon, and it should reproduce it, last commit without the addons directory should be https://github.com/selgesel/godot4-third-person-controller/commit/18d212d1efb33b9014e2824890330d20a2cfdfa2 For the other issues, just copy the content of the addons folder in another project, a version before I fixed the reference. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) This should do it https://github.com/selgesel/godot4-third-person-controller/commit/18d212d1efb33b9014e2824890330d20a2cfdfa2

title: DOF broken in mobile renderer
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in: 4.4.1.stable, 4.5.rc1



### System information

Windows 11, Godot 4.4.1.stable/4.5.rc1, NVIDIA Geforce RTX 4070

### Issue description

DOF (Depth of Field) appears to be broken in 4.4.1.stable / 4.5.rc1 with Mobile renderer. It works fine with Forward+ renderer.

"Broken" means that the distance settings have no effect, everything is either always blurred or not blurred. It also depends if you choose far or near, I think for near if you set like 4000m it stops being blurred suddenly.

The funny thing is that the preview window for the Camera3D node inside the inspector panel shows everything correctly(!). Just in the running game it is not working. 

I still hope I am just missing something here, but I guess its another mobile bug.

**EDIT:** I investigated a bit more and I figured out that if I **disable MSAA** it works with Mobile renderer. Maybe this is intended for performance reasons or something? Could imagine some issues here. I don't necessarily need MSAA on a mobile device I think. Good enough if it works on Forward+. But I think it should be documented and perhaps there should be a warning popping up: "DOF doesn't work with MSAA enabled on Mobile Renderer" or something.

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP with Mobile renderer, select the "main.tscn" scene and run the scene. You will see that all the cubes are clearly visible. If you check the inspector preview of the Camera3D node, you will see that half of the cubes are blurred as it actually should be in the game too. If you switch to Forward+ you will see everything is fine.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[dof_bug_mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22253593/dof_bug_mrp.zip)

title: Using OS.move_to_trash causes graphical glitch
body:
### Tested versions

v3.7.dev.custom_build [5155fe5a4]

### System information

win11

### Issue description

In my project, I was using OS.move_to_trash to delete save files.
I have always tested my project in windowed mode (not maximized).
Today I maximized the window and noticed a graphical issue.

Upon investigation, I discovered that it occurs when calling OS.move_to_trash while the game window is maximized. The glitch does not happen when the window is minimized.

I modified the code to use Directory to delete the files instead, and the issue was resolved.

Video showing the error:

https://youtu.be/dCxHqSkZviw?si=edFvWWTx9Qbw_HPd&t=5

Pause the video at 0:05
Step frame by frame using the "." (period) key
You can see strange flickering and menu letters changing rapidly inappropriately. This does not happen in the code itself; something unusual happens graphically when calling OS.move_to_trash.

Video without the error:

https://youtu.be/5ESjgRgcrUw?si=FZNyf0DLKTzM_ny6

The issue occurs when I press the button and select the option "USED" in the menu, which then calls OS.move_to_trash.
Interestingly, the glitch happens when the window is maximized, but it does not occur if the window is not maximized.
It’s like Godot chokes when OS.move_to_trash is called.

author: BlendyDev

title: Resource silently fails to load with specific combination of @export/preload/globals/script references
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] and v4.5.rc1.official [08e9e6b29]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7283) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9700K CPU @ 3.60GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

With a very specific set of Resource subclasses, use of preload, globals, scenes, and references in script, a custom resource value assigned to an @export variable will silently fail to load (and instead simply be null) when the game is run in editor. No modification to the scene or files occurs and the assigned value is still correctly set when returning to the editor.

Crucially, the specific arrangement of classes, scenes, scripts, etc that are present all seem to be necessary to trigger this bug. If you remove _any_ part of the MRP, the bug behaviour goes away and the resource loads correctly. I do mean _any_ part of the MRP. It can be using a class name in print statement. It can be the order of globals loading. It can be removing an unused argument or the empty resource subclass. There is a chain established that during parsing, if done in a specific order, reaches a class that has a preload() call for a scene, which itself includes an instance of the original scene.

That sounds like a cyclic reference in scenes/resources but I don't see anything structurally wrong with it. Resources do not appear to have cyclic references, nor do scenes. The scripts certainly do refer to each other in a cyclical way, which seems to be causing an issue when the preload() is hit.

This is similar to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/95909 in that I have again stumbled on to some architecture that elicits a failure to load custom Resources. However, this issue does not have any console output or errors at any time, nor does it delete any data.




### Steps to reproduce

Run the MRP to see the specific, fragile structure needed to elicit the bug.

When you run the main scene ("my_class.tscn"), you will see it hits an assert as the Resource loaded is null (incorrectly). If you change any part of the project, the resource will load correctly.

Here is an example showing the resource failing to load, followed by making an innocuous change and the resource loading correctly:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d5edd9b9-6e91-4f64-9b92-8baab7ad201a

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[2025-09-10-null-resource-bug-clean.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22261827/2025-09-10-null-resource-bug-clean.zip)

title: Blendspace2D doesn't update the animation when blend_position is set
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1, 4.5.beta7, 4.5.rc2 
- Not reproducible in: 4.3

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Linux Mint 21.3 (Virginia) - X11 - GLES3 (Compatibility) - REMBRANDT (rembrandt, LLVM 15.0.7, DRM 3.42, 5.15.0-151-generic) - AMD Ryzen 5 PRO 6650U with Radeon Graphics (12 Threads)

### Issue description

I'm using an AnimationTree to control the animations for an AnimatedSprite2D using an AnimationPlayer. I have a state machine as the root node and I'm using BlendSpace2D nodes as the leaf nodes so I can set the animation based on the direction of the player by setting the blend_space parameter to match the player's normalised movement vector. After upgrading to the 4.5 beta I found that setting the blend_space parameter doesn't change the animation.

In my original project I found that the animation changes eventually (after one or more seconds of holding a different direction) but couldn't reproduce that in an MRP.

### Steps to reproduce

Using the MRP:
1) Move the character around using the arrow keys.

Expected behaviour (seen in 4.3):
* The animation changes when changing direction.

Actual behaviour (seen in 4.4.1 and later):
* The animation doesn't change.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-blendspace2d.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22267035/test-blendspace2d.zip)

author: fstxz

title: SeparationRayShape3D separation is applied 'late', allowing visible clipping
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.4.1 stable and 4.5 rc2

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8115) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11800H @ 2.30GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

It seems like the SeperationRayShape3D is not being taken into account during actual movement and collision, and is applied 'late', allowing the object's position before the SeperationRayShape3D takes effect to be visible. 
This also causes issues even if steps are taken to avoid having velocity that moves into objects - e.g with a CharacterBody3D, if gravity is only applied when is_on_floor returns false, this behaviour results in the character visibly clipping into the ground and snapping back up. 
The primary issue is the 'late' application of the separation which makes the clipping visible, but in general I believe if SeperationRayShape3D is intended to have identical behaviour to a ray cast which moves the object back when it hits something and only updates once per physics tick, it shouldn't be presented as a collision shape, as all other collision shapes actually have their collision resolved every time move_and_collide is called. If this isn't the intended behaviour, then it should be made consistent with other collision shapes.

### Steps to reproduce

I've provided a project to demonstrate the issue, just run it and press Tab to toggle the object moving downwards. You'll be able to see the cylinder object clipping through the plane when it has downwards velocity.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[reproduction_project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22271792/reproduction_project.zip)

author: SkanerSoft

title: Accessibility: Node rename popup in Scene dock lacks screen reader feedback
body:
### Tested versions

v4.5.rc2

### System information

Windows 10, NVDA screen reader v2024.4.2

### Issue description

The popup/text field for renaming a node in the Scene dock is not accessible to screen readers.

When a user selects a node and presses `F2` or `Enter` to rename it, a text field appears. While typing works visually, the screen reader (NVDA) provides no spoken feedback for the characters being typed or for the text field itself. This makes it impossible for visually impaired users to verify their input or use this core feature effectively.

This appears to be a regression, as this functionality was accessible in version `4.5.dev3`.

For comparison, the search box in the "Add Node" dialog **is** accessible and provides the expected spoken feedback, suggesting this is an issue specific to the Scene dock's rename implementation.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Launch Godot with a screen reader (e.g., NVDA on Windows) running.
2. Create a new project or open an existing one.
3. In the **Scene** dock, select any node (e.g., the default root node).
4. Press `F2` or `Enter` to trigger the rename action.
5. Attempt to type a new name for the node.

**Expected result:** The screen reader should announce the text field is active and speak each character as it is typed.
**Actual result:** The text is updated visually in the editor, but the screen reader provides no auditory feedback, making the input "blind."

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This is an editor accessibility issue and can be reproduced in any project, including a new, empty one. A minimal reproduction project is not required.
author: Unique-Creators

title: Accessibility: Focus is not transferred to the 'Import Project' dialog
body:
### Tested versions

v4.5.rc2 (Also observed in v4.5.dev7)

### System information

Windows 10, NVDA screen reader v2024.4.2

### Issue description

When the Project Manager list is empty and a user chooses to import a project, the keyboard and screen reader focus does not correctly move to the newly opened 'Import' dialog.

After selecting a `.project.godot` file, the import dialog appears, but the focus remains stuck on the 'Import' button in the main Project Manager window. Consequently, keyboard input (like `Tab`, `Enter`, or typing) does not register in the new dialog, making it completely unusable for anyone relying on keyboard-only navigation or a screen reader.

The only workaround is to use the mouse to manually click on the import dialog, at which point focus is correctly transferred and it becomes usable. This presents a major barrier for users who cannot use a mouse.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Ensure no projects are currently listed in the Godot Project Manager. (This is crucial, the bug occurs when the list is empty).
2. Launch the Godot Project Manager with a screen reader running.
3. Navigate to and activate the "Import" button.
4. The system's file browser will open. Select a valid `.project.godot` file and click "Open".
5. The 'Import Project' dialog now appears on top of the Project Manager.

**Expected result:** The keyboard and screen reader focus should automatically move to the new 'Import Project' dialog, allowing the user to interact with its elements (like the "Import & Edit" button) immediately via the keyboard.
**Actual result:** The 'Import Project' dialog appears, but the focus remains on the "Import" button in the background window. No keyboard interaction with the new dialog is possible until it is manually clicked with a mouse.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This is an issue with the Project Manager's UI and does not depend on the contents of the project being imported. Any valid Godot 4 project can be used to trigger this bug. One can test this by creating a new blank project, closing it, removing it from the list, and then attempting to import it.

author: Maran23

title: Split viewport shortcuts don't work when emulate numpad is true
body:
### Tested versions

In 4.3.x, where `editors/3d/navigation/emulate_numpad` was disabled by default, splitting viewports by shortcut worked fine.  In 4.4.x, `editors/3d/navigation/emulate_numpad` is true by default, and splitting viewports only works via the UI.
The default was changed in: a9881f99a027f1bdbe65dc1b7e561bc49b744a58

### System information

Windows 10 - 4.4.1, 4.5.rc2. TKL Keyboard; ENG-INTL Keyboard layout.

### Issue description

Pressing `CTRL+2` does not split the viewport when `editors/3d/navigation/emulate_numpad` is `true`. Similar shortcuts are also affected.

This is because the numpad emulation changes the input event to a keypad event not locally but modifies the original event.
It seems, `p_event` being marked `const` is bypassed by the assignment of `Ref<InputEventKey> k = p_event;`, and upon which `set_keycode` is later called.
I have not checked, but it might be worth investigating whether this is being done in other locations, as the `const` guard is essentially violated.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open 3D view. 
- Press Ctrl+2. 
- Observe that viewports don't split properly.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Can be reproduced in empty project.

### Issue description

EDIT: Just realized might be related to: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/106078

If you set Direct3D 12 as the rendering backend in settings, the CollisionShape3D instances have no visible handles to resize them. Happens with Mobile and Forward+. With Vulkan as rendering backend, the handles are visible. 

I wonder why this happens and if something like that could also happen in-game with D3D12 backend so that certain game elements suddently become invisible with D3D12. I hope not, so far everything is fine. (I usually use D3D12 as default to by pass glitches in Vulkan drivers on Windows)

title: start_drag() does not trigger OS window tiling behaviour on Windows 11 when window is borderless
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in 
- v4.5.rc2.official [2dd26a027]
- v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]


### System information

Godot v4.5.rc2 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 7600 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.13036.4) - AMD Ryzen 7 9700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.13 GiB memory

### Issue description

The intended behaviour for `Window.start_drag()` is for the window to act as if it were being dragged on the titlebar, with the OS behaving as such. This is not the case when using a borderless window with the Windows 11 OS, though I have tested this on Linux using KDE Plasma 6 (Wayland) and it works as it should. Have not tested on Windows 10.

### Steps to reproduce

In a project set the window to be borderless in the project settings then call `get_window().start_drag()` from some event, say a Control button being pressed, and drag the window to the top of the screen. The Windows 11 tiling behaviour will not be triggered.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

MRP not needed but here is a bare-bones project just for ease.

[start_drag_bug_MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22283744/start_drag_bug_MRP.zip)

title: [outline_size=0] BBCode parameter produces error
body:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4.1 Stable

### System information

MacOS 15.6.1 | M4 Pro MacBook Pro 24GB 

### Issue description

I kept getting an error when working on a scene with several RichTextLabels:

**ERROR: scene/gui/rich_text_label.cpp:4139 - Parameter "current->parent" is null.**

I did some debugging and found out the problem line was `[outline_size=0]`. When I changed it to `[outline_size=1]` the error goes away.

If this isn't a bug then maybe the documentation needs to be updated. Since in the Inspector I can set the Font Outline Size to 0, I'd assume the same would be true in BBCode. I should also add that `[outline_size=0]` does work, but it spawns the error as well.

For full documentation purposes, this is the error in the debugger when I run the scene with `[outline_size=0]`:

```
E 0:00:00:915   pop: Parameter "current->parent" is null.
  <C++ Source>  scene/gui/rich_text_label.cpp:4139 @ pop()
```

Seems mildly similar to [this issue](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/84243).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a RichTextLabel to a Scene
2. Enable BBCode
3. Add the line: `[outline_size=0][/outline_size]`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[error-project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22289030/error-project.zip)
author: Drinkingpants74

title: WebRtcMultiplayerPeer does not notify peers when calling close()
body:
### Tested versions

4.4.1.stable.mono

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

When setting up a basic multiplayer example using the `WebRtcMultiplayerPeer`, calling `Close()` on a connected instance does not notify other connected peers via the `PeerDisconnected` signal the way it does with `ENetMultiplayerPeer`.  Godot does finally send the `PeerDisconnected` signal when exiting the game which makes me feel more like this is a bug.

I didn't do a deep dive but just comparing the two close methods, it looks like the `ENetMultiplayerPeer` loops through and disconnects each peer while the WebRtc version does not:

[webrtc_multiplayer_peer,cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/modules/webrtc/webrtc_multiplayer_peer.cpp#L440-L449)

[enet_multiplayer_peer.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/master/modules/enet/enet_multiplayer_peer.cpp#L290-L314)

The current workaround is to manually disconnect each peer when the user wants to disconnect:
```c#
if (Multiplayer.HasMultiplayerPeer())
{
  foreach (var id in Multiplayer.GetPeers())
  {
    Multiplayer.MultiplayerPeer.DisconnectPeer(id);
  }
}
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Establish a connection between two game clients with `Multiplayer.MultiplayerPeer` set as an instance of `WebRtcMultiplayerPeer`.
2. Call `Close()` on either side.
3. Notice how the other side's `Multiplayer.PeerDisconnected` signal never fires.
4. Close the game and notice how the other side finally sees the disconnect.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

- To run the desktop version, you will need to download the GDExtension version of [webrtc-native](https://github.com/godotengine/webrtc-native/releases) and extract it to the project root.  Adding it to the MRP makes it too large for me to upload here 😕 
- It's pointing at a test server that will need to be woken up first.  You may have to click host a couple of times before it works.

[webrtcmrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22290316/webrtcmrp.zip)
author: dtgreene

title: Better Rendering Improvement in Godot v4.6
body: ### Tested versions I noticed an issue in Godot 4.5 related to the PBR material system. When **Roughness is set to the lowest value**, the material **does not properly reflect the surrounding environment**. Even with **Screen Space Reflections (SSR) enabled**, the reflection is extremely limited and only affects nearby objects. In comparison, in Blender, setting **Metallic high** and **Roughness low** produces a **full mirror-like effect**. Godot’s current approach reflects mostly the environment and directional light, but not the full scene or background. This can make players feel **disoriented in closed spaces** during gameplay, since reflections do not match what they expect from a realistic mirror surface. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Create a PBR material on a mesh. 2. Set **Metallic** to 1.0 (or high). 3. Set **Roughness** to 0.0 (lowest). 4. Enable **Screen Space Reflections** in the Environment node. 5. Observe that **distant objects are barely reflected**. ## Expected Behavior - Low Roughness + High Metallic should produce a **near-perfect mirror reflection** similar to Blender’s Cycles/Eevee output. - Reflection should **not be limited to nearby objects** only. - Optional: Support for **Planar Reflections** or **CubeMap reflections** to simulate full mirror effects in real-time. ## Additional Notes - This could **significantly improve the realism** of reflective surfaces in games and simulations. - Potential solutions: Integrate or enhance **Planar Reflection nodes, CubeMap reflections, or ray-traced reflections**. ## Screenshot Comparison ### Godot ![Godot Reflection Example](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7bb53d63-1a4f-4c13-a3d4-bbfd7d9ea1f9) ### Blender ![Blender Reflection Example](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c1106ef6-8424-4f69-8272-4e410021bf8a) ### System information Godot v4.5.dev4.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated Intel(R) Arc(TM) A380 Graphics (Intel Corporation; 32.0.101.5971) - Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E3-1231 v3 @ 3.40GHz (8 threads) ### Issue description In Godot 4.5, I noticed an issue with the PBR material system. When Roughness is set to the lowest value, the material does not properly reflect the surrounding environment. Even with Screen Space Reflections (SSR) enabled, reflections are very limited and only affect nearby objects. In comparison, in Blender, setting Metallic high and Roughness low produces a full mirror-like reflection. Godot’s current implementation mainly reflects the environment and directional light, but not the full scene or background. This can cause players to feel disoriented in closed spaces, because reflections do not match the expected realistic mirror effect. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a new 3D scene in Godot 4.6. 2. Add a MeshInstance3D (e.g., Cube or Plane) to the scene. 3. Create a new PBRMaterial for the mesh. 4. Set the material parameters: ``` gdscript material.metallic = 1.0 # high metallic material.roughness = 0.0 # lowest roughness ``` * or in the inspector 5. Add an Environment node and enable Screen Space Reflections (SSR). 6. Add other objects at various distances in the scene. 7. Run the scene and observe: * Distant objects are barely reflected. * Reflection behaves differently than in Blender (mirror-like effect missing). Following these steps, you will notice that the reflections only show distant environment and directional light (sun), but **do not properly reflect nearby objects or scene elements**. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) This is my sample project to explain the godot users [the-sample-project-for-the-glass-mat.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22290964/the-sample-project-for-the-glass-mat.zip)

title: TileSet Physics Layer interface missing in 4.5 RC2 Ubuntu 22.04
body:
### Tested versions

This wasn't in any of the Beta versions (I used them up until the latest), but it was definitely here for RC2. (I didn't try RC1)  Specifically I am using v4.5.rc2.official [2dd26a027]

The Physics Layer 0 section is just completely missing from the TileSet editor. And, on a related note...any time any RigidBody2D connects with a TileMapLayer wall, the RigidBody2D remains in 'existence,' but its position becomes nan (not a number).

I would reproduce this for you in a minimal project, but..."This isn't in the editor anymore" isn't something I can really "show" in a project.  And the related issue, I can't put in a minimal project, because I can't define a layer enough to make the minimal project where anything collides (due to the missing controls). 

I could paste in my TileSet .tres, but...I don't know what good that would really do. It was definitely working in some of the beta versions before any of the RCs.  But...It doesn't seem to be working in past versions now. I am very confused, and am afraid some of my .tres files have been changed when upgrading.

I think this might be a weird Linux-specific regression, and I think these might be relevant articles:

#78848 (Cannot Add Tileset Physics Layer)
#117160 (Physics layers missing in 4.4)

### System information

Ubuntu 22.04, v4.5.rc2.official [2dd26a027]

### Issue description

The controls simply aren't present.

Also, whenever any RigidBody2D touches a TileMapLayer physics body, the RigidBody's position becomes nan.  No matter how barely / softly. Debug console output verifies that it'll do it when it's moving very very slowly, and only a single contact point (so it's not like it's somehow 'wiggling' between bits).  It happens the very instant that they touch.

I reduced the physics_quadrant_size to 1, in case that mattered, and it was identical behavior to 16 (the default).
Checking "use_kinematic_bodies" also had no effect.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Be on Ubuntu 22.04 and run 4.5rc2
2. Try to edit the TileMap
3. You did it!

On the related issue, you would have to already have a TileMapLayer and its requisite TileSet, because you can't even edit that resource enough to give it physics shapes so that you can let things connect. So...I guess...
1. Be on Ubuntu 22.04 and run 4.5rc2 in a project where you already have a TileMapLayer with physics objects
2. Let any RigidBody2D touch it for any reason, at any speed or angle.
3. Watch the RigidBody2D instantly disappear, but still be able to give debugging reports, like its position being (nan,nan)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)


Any new project with a TileMapLayer + TileSet should do it.

[minimal.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22291361/minimal.zip)

title: Inconsistent newline shortcuts.
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5-rc2, 4.4.1-stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc2 - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV RENOIR) - AMD Ryzen 5 5500U with Radeon Graphics (12 threads) - 7.11 GiB memory

### Issue description

The shortcuts used in the GDScript editor feel inconsistent and i'm not sure if this is intended.

There's a shortcut `Ctrl+Enter` named `ui_text_newline_blank` and nothing blank happens about that newline in the GDScript editor.

Currently pressing `Enter` either doesn't make a newline when combined with other keys, or makes a newline the same exact way when using `Enter` or `Ctrl+Enter`, preserving tabulation. And `Ctrl+Shirt+Enter` adds a newline above, preserving tabulation.

---

A behavior i would expect is more consistent.

Let's take `Tab` as one example.

`Tab` indents, `Shift+Tab` unindents. So Shift is the reverse of the keybind. This means the reverse of `Enter` should just be `Shift+Enter` and not `Ctrl+Shift+Enter`.

Let's take arrow keys as another example.

`Shift` with arrow keys selects text. `Ctrl` with arrow keys moves the cursor through tokens or words, or scrolls one line up/down. `Alt` with arrow keys moves text up or down, and left-right swaps me between cursor history similar to the mouse side buttons (iirc button 4 and 5?).

This means each modifier key has its own function.

---

So to make it all consistent, i suggest to use `Enter` in the following way:
- `Shift` adds a newline above, preserving indentation.
- `Ctrl` adds a blank newline. so indentation is not preserved.
- `Ctrl`+`Shift` naturally combines to add a blank newline above.
- Something for `Alt` maybe?

### Steps to reproduce

New project, open GDScript editor, use shortcuts.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N-A

title: [iOS] Third-party keyboards (e.g. WeChat Keyboard) cannot be used in Godot apps
body:
### Tested versions

4.3stable

### System information

iOS version: (e.g. iOS 17.5)

### Issue description

On iOS, Godot apps cannot seem to use third-party keyboards (such as the WeChat Keyboard).
When focusing a LineEdit, TextEdit, or even a native UITextField via a custom plugin, only the system default keyboard shows up. The user cannot switch to any third-party keyboards, even though they are enabled in system settings.For non-English regions (e.g. Chinese users), third-party keyboards like WeChat Keyboard are very important. Many users rely on them for typing, predictive input, or advanced IME features. Without support for third-party keyboards, text input in Godot apps becomes a major UX issue.

### Steps to reproduce

Install a third-party keyboard (e.g. WeChat Keyboard) on iOS.

Open a Godot app (tested with 4.3 and 4.4).

Focus on a text input field (Godot LineEdit, TextEdit, or a plugin-created UITextField).

Try switching keyboards.

Only the default iOS keyboard appears; the third-party one is not available.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Minimal reproduction project:  
A brand new Godot 4.4 project with a single LineEdit node.  
Export to iOS, install WeChat Keyboard on the device, and try to switch keyboards when focusing the LineEdit.  
Only the system default keyboard is available.

author: shiena

title: RDShaderFile not updating when edited in external editor, despite explicit load with ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in 4.5-rc2, 4.4.1

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc2 - macOS Sonoma (14.7.8) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (16 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

I'm working on a glsl shader used in a CompositorEffect, and want to hot-reload the shader as I make changes in an eternal editor.  I got all the code working, only to discover, the GLSL is not reloaded unless I explicitly switch back to the godot editor, despite telling ResourceLoader to ignore the cache:

```gdscript
    var shader_file: RDShaderFile = ResourceLoader.load(glsl_shader_file, "", ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE)
```

When I save changes to the glsl file, my `_reload_shader()` compiling the shader and recreating the pipeline, but it doesn't visually update.  But, if I click over to the Godot Editor, I can see it reload the shader, then go back to my editor and save again, the shader updates.

Also, the updated shader is not the most recent version on disk; it's the one that was loaded when I clicked over into the godot editor.

You can see this happen in the video below.  I start by changing the color of the triangle from blue to green in the shader.  When I save the shader, the console shows the shader and pipeline are rebuilt, but the triangle stays blue on screen.  I then click over to godot, then back to my editor, and set the triangle color to red, and save to disk.  At this time, the pipeline rebuilds, and the visual changes to show **the green** triangle; which was the state it was in the last time I clicked on the editor.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/363c8ae6-691f-49c8-97f0-154420080210

Is this the intended behavior?  Is there a way to disable the caching so I can get hot-reload working?

### Steps to reproduce

* Start the attached project
* In an external editor make a change to outline.glsl, and save the file
* Note that the change does not appear in the game window
* Click over to the godot editor
* Switch back to external editor and save again
* Note that the change appears in the game window

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Project is here: [rd_shader_file-caching_issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22305036/rd_shader_file-caching_issue.zip)

It's minimal-ish because I didn't want to have to rebuild the whole example outside of a CompositorEffect.  The key function is in `outline_compositor_effect.gd` to reload the shader.  It is triggered by the scene root `_process()` detecting that the shader file has changed.

```gdscript
func _reload_shader():
    print("rebuilding shader")

    # destroy old shader if present
    if shader.is_valid():
        rd.free_rid(shader)
        shader = RID()
        pipeline = RID()


    # load the shader
    # XXX The issue is here: if you edit the shader in an external editor, the
    #     changes are not loaded, despite ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE.
    #     You have to switch into a godot window and resave before the most
    #     recent version is loaded.
    var shader_file: RDShaderFile = ResourceLoader.load(glsl_shader_file, "", ResourceLoader.CACHE_MODE_IGNORE)
    var shader_spirv: RDShaderSPIRV = shader_file.get_spirv()
    if shader_file.base_error != "":
        push_error(shader_file.base_error)
        return
    if shader_spirv.compile_error_vertex != "":
        push_error(shader_spirv.compile_error_vertex)
        return
    if shader_spirv.compile_error_fragment != "":
        push_error(shader_spirv.compile_error_fragment)
        return
    shader = rd.shader_create_from_spirv(shader_spirv)

    #create the pipeline
    var blend := RDPipelineColorBlendState.new()
    var blend_attachment := RDPipelineColorBlendStateAttachment.new()
    blend.attachments.push_back(blend_attachment)
    pipeline = rd.render_pipeline_create(
        shader,
        rd.screen_get_framebuffer_format(),
        vertex_format,
        RenderingDevice.RENDER_PRIMITIVE_TRIANGLES,
        RDPipelineRasterizationState.new(),
        RDPipelineMultisampleState.new(),
        RDPipelineDepthStencilState.new(),
        blend
    )
    print("new pipeline ", pipeline)
```

title: VSync Jitter with dual GPU in Windows 11
body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.4.1-stable, 4.5-stable ### System information Lenovo Slim 7 Pro X, 6900HS w/ Nvidia 3050, Windows 11, Godot 4.4.1-stable, 3440x1440 external monitor ### Issue description I ran into a bug while working on a scroll carousel. I was getting extreme jitter when moving it via a Tween. The objects would occasionally appear to "jump back" a few frames before continuing on their path as expected. The code was outputting correct values for position, it was only the visual that appeared to be incorrect (not smooth). I was not able to recreate the effect on an android phone or my desktop and the effect seemed random. So I recreated the bug in the attached project with simplified code, and still had the issue. I noticed it looked kind of like screen tearing with the simplified blocks, so I disabled VSync in the editor settings, and my original project worked! I was confused as to why this was the case, so I dug a little deeper and I felt I should report back here as there appears to be a bug in how Godot handles something (but what -- that is beyond my ability). I tested about 50 different configurations and determined a few key things that each independently fixed it. 1. Turning off VSync in the Godot Editor Project Settings. 2. Use Compatibility Renderer (other two still have bug). 3. Undock laptop from the external monitor and use the attached display (this will make sense in a little bit) 4. Add Godot to Windows custom video settings (System -> Display -> Graphics) and turning OFF "Let Windows Decide" and to select the current display Driver that was attached to the current display (System -> Display -> Advanced Display) It think the issue ultimately relates to the fact that when my laptop is connected to an external display, it automatically decides to output the graphics through the installed AMD driver, regardless of what GPU is actually running on the system. When Windows starts up Godot while docked, it will ALWAYS select NVidia 3050 to run Godot when I use the default "Let Windows decide". This selection is made by Windows regardless of what driver is actually outputting the display to the external monitor. This can be confirmed several ways, especially in the output when I run the project. This mismatch between the GPU that Godot is rendering in and the actual output appears to be the source of the problem. Manually overriding this Windows setting to force it to run Godot on the AMD GPU fixes the Sync issue. This problem does not occur if I undock the laptop (with either GPU, in any circumstance). l suspect when this happens, the laptop outputs directly to the attached screen, bypassing whatever relay happens when I output via USB-C. This relay is introducing the desync with VSync and screen tearing. I was unable to remove it by changing any of the settings in the NVidia Control Panel. Godot appears to be able to detect this disconnect when the output is going directly to the hard-wired monitor. If I'm undocked and output my display to the AMD driver, but run Godot in NVidia, this issue does not appear. It ONLY appears when I display to an external monitor. Here are the two displays in question, the Laptop display is the larger resolution with higher refresh rate: I suspect this issue may be related to a few others that I have seen posted about VSync jitter. It appears to be an OS level issue -- or at least the OS communication and how Godot interprets it. I don't really know enough about how Godot detects the framerate or Vsync capabilities of the output, so I'm limited in my ability to assist beyond testing. However, it's clear that Windows is assigning Godot to run on a GPU and providing Godot incorrect information and/or settings that Godot trusts. However, this output is being rerouted to a display with different settings, causing jitter/tearing. ### Steps to reproduce I'm not sure how this can be reproduced without the specific laptop model I have. I'm unsure how deep into the hardware this goes or if this is a Windows limitation. But I suspect any system which can trick windows into displaying through one GPU and allowing Windows to decide the GPU to run Godot on, and Windows selects the other, would be sufficient. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [test_scroll.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22306189/test_scroll.zip)

title: _ready called twice on custom bottom_panel controls
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.rc2, 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Windows 11, Godot Engine v4.5.rc2.officia, Forward+ - dedicated geforce 4080 gtx

### Issue description

Controls added with [EditorPlugin.add_control_to_bottom_panel](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/4.4/classes/class_editorplugin.html#class-editorplugin-method-add-control-to-bottom-panel) in an addons `enter_tree` function have their `_ready` function called twice.

This is a problem since bottom panels often connect signals in their _ready and if signals are connected multiple times it can result in undesired behaviour.

Note that `@onready` calls are also affected.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create an addon with the following:
```gdscript
# myplugin.gd
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

var mypanel: MyPanel

func _enter_tree() -> void:
	print("entering tree")
	mypanel = load("res://addons/bottompaneltest/my_panel.tscn").instantiate()
	add_control_to_bottom_panel(mypanel, "My Panel")


func _exit_tree() -> void:
	remove_control_from_bottom_panel(mypanel)

# my_panel.gd
@tool
extends Control
class_name MyPanel

func _ready() -> void:
	print("_ready called")

```
- Restart the editor

You'll see the following output:
> entering tree
_ready called
_ready called


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

MRP: [bottom-panel-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22308951/bottom-panel-test.zip)

title: Imported AnimationLibrary read-only handling is incorrect after adding extra AnimationLibrary into AnimationMixer
body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5 ### System information Any ### Issue description Imported Animations should not be edited, as their state reverts each time they are re-imported. The default Global (empty string) AnimationLibrary immediately after import correctly handles read-only. However, adding an AnimationLibrary to the AnimationMixer containing it breaks the read-only handling. This changes its state from "foreign" to "built-in", making it editable. Although the track has a separate imported flag that triggers the warning below, all operations are permitted in the GUI. Looking at the source code, it appears to determine this by checking whether `.import` exists in the resource path's filesystem. However, since the path changed after adding the AnimationLibrary, the determination seems to be failing. This issue has existed since version 4.1, prior to the AnimationMixer implementation, so it likely occured with the initial implementation of the AnimationLibrary. Disabling the ManageAnimationLibrary option for imported AnimationPlayers is the simplest solution, but I'm concerned about breaking compatibility. Another potential fix involves preserving the entity paths of Animations within the modified AnimationLibraryList, though I'm unsure if this is feasible. ### Steps to reproduce Add the AnimationLibrary to the imported AnimationMixer, save the .tscn file, and reopen it. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [import_animlib.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22309417/import_animlib.zip)

title: `RETURN_VALUE_DISCARDED` warning raised when using any function call as `match` pattern guard
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.rc2

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc2 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - AMD Ryzen 7 7745HX with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 63.18 GiB memory

### Issue description

With debug/gdscript/warnings/return_value_discarded set to warning or error, using a function call as the pattern guard in a match statement triggers the warning.
For example
```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:
    match name:
        "Node" when is_inside_tree(): # (RETURN_VALUE_DISCARDED): The function "is_inside_tree()" returns a value that will be discarded if not used.
            pass
```
Ideally a pattern guard should consume the return value and not raise a warning or error.

### Steps to reproduce

Take the match statement in the previous code snippet and add it to any function in any project

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22310873/example.zip)

title: Duplicating `ShaderMaterial` reverts certain shader parameters
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.5.rc.double.custom_build [bfa330dd5]

Did not test in any other version yet.

### System information

Godot v4.5.rc.double (bfa330dd5) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8129) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12400F (12 threads) - 31.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

Duplicating a ShaderMaterial with modified shader parameters that were set by code (not in the editor) will reset certain types back to the "default" (editor) values for both the original and the duplicated ShaderMaterial.

For example, if you set a shader parameter to be a certain Vector2, and then duplicate the material, the original and the duplicate materials with both now revert this Vector2 to the one that can be seen in the editor, i.e. the parameter that was just set is gone, even for the original material!

Simple example: Editor shows Vector2(0,0) for some parameter. You change it to Vector2(1, 1) in code (`set_shader_parameter`), and after duplicating this one material, all instances of that same material will now show Vector2(0,0) again.

I can reproduce this for Vector2 and Vector3 types, but not for float and int for example.

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP and click play, in the console output you will see the originally set parameter and then the values after `mat_a.duplicate()` has been called.

The editor shows Vector2(0,0) for albedo_texture_size. Changing it to Vector2.ONE and printing the parameter is successfull. But after duplicating, the value reverts back to Vector2(0,0). The same is NOT true for the float point_size for example.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[min-repro-shader.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22314547/min-repro-shader.zip)

title: Getting the value of a typed dictionary when the key is a freed object fails and prints an error
body:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6607) - AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 w/ Radeon 890M (24 threads)

### Issue description

If a typed dictionary has a freed object as one of its keys, obtaining that key's value results in an error.

```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	untyped()
	typed()


func untyped() -> void:
	var dict := {}
	var obj := Object.new()
	dict[obj] = 1
	obj.free()
	print(dict) # fine; prints { <Freed Object>: 1 }
	print(dict[obj]) # fine; prints 1


func typed() -> void:
	var dict: Dictionary[Object, int] = {}
	var obj := Object.new()
	dict[obj] = 1
	obj.free()
	print(dict) # error; prints { <Freed Object>: 0 }
	print(dict[obj]) # crash
```

When obtaining the value in `print()`, the following error is printed:
```
E 0:00:01:017   main.gd:23 @ typed(): Attempted to use `operator[]` an invalid (previously freed?) object instance into a 'TypedDictionary.Key'.
  <C++ Error>   Parameter "object" is null.
  <C++ Source>  ./core/variant/container_type_validate.h:122 @ validate_object()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:23 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
```

When obtaining the value directly using the `[]` operator, godot crashes and shows the following error:
```
Invalid access to property or key '<Freed Object>' on a base object of type 'Dictionary[Object, int]'.
```
In addition, the following 9 errors are printed, in addition to the previous error:
```
E 0:00:00:967   main.gd:24 @ typed(): Attempted to getptr an invalid (previously freed?) object instance into a 'TypedDictionary.Key'.
  <C++ Error>   Parameter "object" is null.
  <C++ Source>  ./core/variant/container_type_validate.h:122 @ validate_object()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:24 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:043   main.gd:24 @ typed(): Attempted to getptr an invalid (previously freed?) object instance into a 'TypedDictionary.Key'.
  <C++ Error>   Parameter "object" is null.
  <C++ Source>  ./core/variant/container_type_validate.h:122 @ validate_object()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:24 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:043   main.gd:24 @ typed(): Parameter "value" is null.
  <C++ Source>  core/io/marshalls.cpp:1865 @ encode_variant()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:24 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:043   main.gd:24 @ typed(): Failed to encode variant.
  <C++ Source>  core/debugger/debugger_marshalls.cpp:80 @ serialize()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:24 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:047   validate_object: Attempted to getptr an invalid (previously freed?) object instance into a 'TypedDictionary.Key'.
  <C++ Error>   Parameter "object" is null.
  <C++ Source>  ./core/variant/container_type_validate.h:122 @ validate_object()
E 0:00:01:047   encode_variant: Parameter "value" is null.
  <C++ Source>  core/io/marshalls.cpp:1865 @ encode_variant()
E 0:00:01:048   encode_variant: Condition "err" is true. Returning: err
  <C++ Source>  core/io/marshalls.cpp:1895 @ encode_variant()
E 0:00:01:048   encode_variant: Condition "err" is true. Returning: err
  <C++ Source>  core/io/marshalls.cpp:1895 @ encode_variant()
E 0:00:01:048   _write_out: Condition "err != OK || size > out_buf.size() - 4" is true. Continuing.
  <C++ Source>  core/debugger/remote_debugger_peer.cpp:108 @ _write_out()
```

Note that none of these errors show up for untyped dictionaries.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a typed dictionary with `Object` for its keys (e.g. `var dict: Dictionary[Object, int] = {}`)
2. Add an object to the dictionary.
3. Free the object.
4. Attempt to obtain the dictionary's value corresponding to the freed object.

author: elmarhoppieland

title: Erasing a freed object from a typed array or dictionary fails and prints an error
body:
### Tested versions

v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6607) - AMD Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 w/ Radeon 890M (24 threads)

### Issue description

When trying to erase an object from a typed array or dictionary, Godot fails to erase it and prints an error message. For arrays, the object can only be removed using `remove_at()`. For dictionaries, the object simply cannot be removed.

```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	untyped()
	typed()

func untyped() -> void:
	var dict := {}
	var array := []
	var obj := Object.new()
	
	dict[obj] = 1
	array.append(obj)
	
	obj.free()
	
	dict.erase(obj) # fine
	array.erase(obj) # fine
	
	print(dict) # prints { }
	print(array) # prints []

func typed() -> void:
	var dict: Dictionary[Object, int] = {}
	var array: Array[Object] = []
	var obj := Object.new()
	
	dict[obj] = 1
	array.append(obj)
	
	obj.free()
	
	dict.erase(obj) # error
	array.erase(obj) # error
	
	print(dict) # prints { <Freed Object>: 0 }
	print(array) # prints [<Freed Object>]
	
	array.remove_at(0) # fine
	print(array) # prints []
```

Erasing the freed object from an untyped array or dictionary is fine, and `untyped()` runs without errors. However, when the array or dictionary is typed, calling `erase()` fails and prints the following errors:
```
E 0:00:01:327   main.gd:34 @ typed(): Attempted to erase an invalid (previously freed?) object instance into a 'TypedDictionary.Key'.
  <C++ Error>   Parameter "object" is null.
  <C++ Source>  ./core/variant/container_type_validate.h:122 @ validate_object()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:34 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:327   main.gd:34 @ typed(): Condition "!_p->typed_key.validate(key, "erase")" is true. Returning: false
  <C++ Source>  core/variant/dictionary.cpp:238 @ erase()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:34 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:327   main.gd:35 @ typed(): Attempted to erase an invalid (previously freed?) object instance into a 'TypedArray'.
  <C++ Error>   Parameter "object" is null.
  <C++ Source>  core/variant/container_type_validate.h:122 @ validate_object()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:35 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:327   main.gd:35 @ typed(): Condition "!_p->typed.validate(value, "erase")" is true.
  <C++ Source>  core/variant/array.cpp:328 @ erase()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:35 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
E 0:00:01:327   main.gd:37 @ typed(): Attempted to use `operator[]` an invalid (previously freed?) object instance into a 'TypedDictionary.Key'.
  <C++ Error>   Parameter "object" is null.
  <C++ Source>  ./core/variant/container_type_validate.h:122 @ validate_object()
  <Stack Trace> main.gd:37 @ typed()
                main.gd:6 @ _ready()
```

Note that the last error occurs due to #110509.

This wouldn't be an issue since you're not really supposed to have references to freed objects anyway but the only alternative is creating a new array or dictionary and using `assign()` which isn't ideal.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create an array or dictionary and type it, e.g. `var dict: Dictionary[Object, int] = {}`.
2. Add an object as a key to the dictionary, or append it to the array.
3. Free the object.
4. Try calling `dict.erase(object)` or `array.erase(object)`.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22319382/mrp.zip)

title: Scene inheritance node rename issue
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot Engine v4.4.stable

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot Engine v4.4.stable.official.4c311cbee - https://godotengine.org OpenGL API OpenGL ES 3.0.0 (ANGLE 2.1.1 git hash: 468fcbe033fa) - Compatibility - Using Device: Google Inc. (Intel) - ANGLE (Intel, Intel(R) HD Graphics 5500 (0x00001616) Direct3D11 vs_5_0 ps_5_0, D3D11-20.19.15.5107)

### Issue description

When you rename a node in a parent scene that was used to inherit other scenes through scene instantiation. An error is generated if you updated the scene tree of the child scenes. A fair assumption is that the rename would've propagated in the child scenes as the symbols would've been updated, however this is not the case. Instead, an error is generated and the child's scene tree is updated with the node which was the child of the now renamed node, now, as a renamed orphaned node.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create parent scene

> - **Action (Node)**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - Effects (Node)

2. Instance 2 child scenes through scene inheritance

> - **ChildA** & **ChildB**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - Effects (Node)

3. In ChildB, add new node (Poison) to the Effects node.

> - **ChildB**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - Effects (Node)
>     - Poison (Node) 

		
4. Rename the Effects node in the parent EnemyEffects

> - **Action (Node)**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - EnemyEffects (Node)

5. In ChildA scene, the node is updated correctly. 

> - **ChildA**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - EnemyEffects (Node)

However, in ChildB there is an error.

> - **ChildB**
>   - **Effects#Poison (Node)**
>   - Info (Node)
>   - EnemyEffects (Node)

**Errors**
- The node was renamed however, the child node was removed
- The child node of the prior Effects node was orphaned and its name was updated to its previous parent
- Log Messages
  - ERROR: Node not found: "Effects/Poison" (relative to "ChildB").
  - WARNING: scene/resources/packed_scene.cpp:178 - Parent path 'EnemyEffects' for node 'Poison' has vanished when instantiating: ''.



### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: ``String.chr`` and ``char`` have unreported changes in godot 4.5 + related breaking print bug
body:
### Tested versions

- Breaks in 4.5
- Works in 4.4

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

In godot 4.4 and before, turning the unicode character **0** into a string using ``String.chr`` or ``char`` would produce an empty string. In godot 4.5, doing the same now produces a string with the character that is defined by unicode 0.

This behavior may make more sense, but the engine does not seems to handle strings with this character very well, if you try to print a string containing this character the printed text following the character will disappear. This leads to entire chunks of print statements disappearing which is a huge headache.

Here is an example(from the reproduction project) where I print the string ``Hi`` + *unicode character 0*

In `4.4`;
```
Using string.chr: 'Hi!' Length: 3
Using char: 'Hi!' Length: 3
```

In `4.5`:
```
Using string.chr: 'Hi!
```
(The output unexpectedly stops when it encounters the 0 character)

### Steps to reproduce

Run this script, both in 4.4 and 4.5, or run my project.

```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:
	var text1 : String = "Hi!" + String.chr(0)
	print("Using string.chr: '", text1, "' Length: ", len(text1))
	var text2 : String = "Hi!" + char(0)
	print("Using char: '", text2, "' Length: ", len(text1))
```



If you want to see the print statement issue, only run this:

```gdscript
extends Node

func _ready() -> void:
	print(char(0))
	for i in range(100):
		print(i)
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[is-godot-tweaking.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22354888/is-godot-tweaking.zip)
author: binkiklou

title: Crash when implementing an abstract function with a comment that has at least 13 characters under it.
body: ### Tested versions - Reproduceable in: 4.5.stable ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8097) - AMD Ryzen 7 9700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 61.66 GiB memory ### Issue description Assume the following parent class (parent_class.gd) ```gdscript @abstract extends Resource class_name ParentClass @abstract func _crash() #WWWWWWWWWWWWW ``` Assume a child class (crashing_child_class.gd) ```gdscript extends ParentClass class_name CrashingChildClass ``` Inside the child class, if you attempt to implement the function `_crash` using Godot's autofill, the editor will instantly crash. Note the comment showing up in the autofill. I suspect this is related. I've tested and the comment needs to have 13 characters or more for the crash to occur. It also must be on a new line from the crashing function. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bab9d6b8-08f8-4ef9-b29f-eff54595e5e7) Edit: Crash Logs ``` ================================================================ CrashHandlerException: Program crashed with signal 4 Engine version: Godot Engine v4.5.stable.official (876b290332ec6f2e6d173d08162a02aa7e6ca46d) Dumping the backtrace. Please include this when reporting the bug on: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues [1] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [2] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [3] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [4] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [5] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [6] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [7] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [8] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [9] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [10] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [11] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [12] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [13] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [14] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [15] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [16] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [17] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [18] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [19] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [20] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [21] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable [22] error(-1): no debug info in PE/COFF executable -- END OF C++ BACKTRACE -- ================================================================ ``` ### Steps to reproduce 1. Add the following files to the root of your project. `parent_class.gd` ```gdscript @abstract extends Resource class_name ParentClass @abstract func _crash() #WWWWWWWWWWWWW ``` `crashing_child_class.gd` ```gdscript extends ParentClass class_name CrashingChildClass ``` 2. Open `crashing_child_class.gd` 3. On line 4, type `func _` 4. Use the autofill to implement the function `_crash()` (tab or click) (noting the comment showing up in the autofill) 5. Crash ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [epic-godot-crash-in-4.5-stable.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22354962/epic-godot-crash-in-4.5-stable.zip) EDIT 2: Seems related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/108639, apologies for the potential duplicate.
