title: Error messages when running a project with a custom StyleBox in main theme
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.stable
- Not reproducible in 4.4.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable (876b29033) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

When using a custom `StyleBox` (that is, a script that `extends StyleBox`) in the project's main theme that has an `@export` parameter, running the project prints the following error 8 times:
```
E 0:00:00:851   StyleBoxSomething.gd:5 @ @implicit_new(): Parameter "SceneTree::get_singleton()" is null.
  <C++ Source>  scene/debugger/scene_debugger.cpp:491 @ parse_message()
  <Stack Trace> StyleBoxSomething.gd:5 @ @implicit_new()
```

I only tested this using gdscript to make the custom style box. I did not try using C#.

### Steps to reproduce

In a new project:
1. Make a new script that `extends StyleBox`. Give it a `class_name`.
2. Add an `@export var` to the script. The type doesn't matter, and it doesn't matter if it's used at all.
3. Override the script's `_draw` method (an implementation of `pass` is fine).
4. Make a new theme file.
5. In the Project Settings, set `GUI/Theme/Custom` to point to the new theme file. This step is very important. This error will not reproduce if the theme is set explicitly or if using a Theme Override.
6. Open the theme editor by double-clicking on the theme file in the FileSystem.
7. Add a new theme for a `Panel`.
8. Make its `panel` property (under StyleBoxes) use the new StyleBox.
9. Make a new scene file. Add a `Panel` as a child.
10. Run the scene. See the errors printed in the debugger panel.

title: Abstract functions will have comments when they are completed during inheritance
body: ### Tested versions v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 with Max-Q Design (NVIDIA; 31.0.15.1700) - AMD Ryzen 7 4800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 23.37 GiB memory ### Issue description I created a class using abstract class keywords and added some abstract functions, then wrote comments for the functions. When I implemented the inheritance class of this class, I found that when completing abstract methods, I would insert the comments of the next function as completion content into the code. ### Steps to reproduce StateBase.gd ``` gdscript ## StateBase @abstract class_name StateBase extends Node var state_machine: StateMachine ## enter state func enter() -> void: pass ## exit state @abstract func exit() -> void @abstract func process_update(delta: float) -> void ## every physics process @abstract func physical_process_update(delta: float) -> void ``` Another code file: ``` gdscript extends StateBase func pr ``` At this point, a completion prompt will appear with comments for the parent class declaration file. If it is the `enter` method, it will not trigger the problem as it is not an abstract method. If it is the `exit` method, the absence of comments in the next function `process_update` will not trigger an issue. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [godot-test-4.5.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22359960/godot-test-4.5.zip)

title: TextServerFallback fails to build without FreeType Module
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5-stable
- Not reproducible in 4.4.1-stable
- Reproducible in [`738dd2fd101ee0d0aa32314ed5bc4529abc6017a`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/738dd2fd101ee0d0aa32314ed5bc4529abc6017a)
- Not reproducible in [`2a9ff392645f1e1e073d937f1f6fb8feac896b2f`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/2a9ff392645f1e1e073d937f1f6fb8feac896b2f)

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M3 Max - Apple M3 Max (14 threads) - 36.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Building a custom template with the fallback text server, but no FreeType font support, fails.

It looks like all uses of `ffsd->face`, except the affected lines, are fenced in preprocessor statements:

```c++
// modules/text_server_fb/text_server_fb.cpp
#ifdef MODULE_FREETYPE_ENABLED
	if (ffsd->face) {
		return; // Do not override scale for dynamic fonts, it's calculated automatically.
	}
#endif
```

According to the module config, freetype is an `optional` dep for the fallback server.


```python
# modules/text_server_fb/config.py
def can_build(env, platform):
    env.module_add_dependencies("text_server_fb", ["freetype", "msdfgen", "svg"], True)
    return True
```

Which would indicate that FreeType is an optional dependency of the fallback server.  

### Steps to reproduce

The following `profile.py` :

```python
module_text_server_adv_enabled = "no"
module_text_server_fb_enabled = "yes"
module_freetype_enabled = "no"
```

With this command:

```shell
# any other platform will do
scons platform=macos target=template_debug profile=profile.py
```

Yields these issues:

```
Building for platform "macos", architecture "arm64", target "template_debug".
WARNING: Disabling 'msdfgen' module as the following dependencies are not satisfied: freetype
scons: done reading SConscript files.
scons: Building targets ...
[  7%] Compiling modules/text_server_fb/text_server_fb.cpp ...
[ 18%] modules/text_server_fb/text_server_fb.cpp:4472:40: error: no member named 'face' in 'TextServerFallback::FontForSizeFallback'
 4472 |                                                         if (ffsd && (FT_HAS_COLOR(ffsd->face) || !FT_IS_SCALABLE(ffsd->face))) {
      |                                                                                   ~~~~  ^
modules/text_server_fb/text_server_fb.cpp:4472:71: error: no member named 'face' in 'TextServerFallback::FontForSizeFallback'                                                                                  
 4472 |                                                         if (ffsd && (FT_HAS_COLOR(ffsd->face) || !FT_IS_SCALABLE(ffsd->face))) {
      |                                                                                                                  ~~~~  ^
[ 19%] 2 errors generated.                                                                                                                                                                                     
[ 65%] Generating core/version_hash.gen.cpp ...
scons: *** [bin/obj/modules/text_server_fb/text_server_fb.macos.template_debug.arm64.o] Error 1
scons: building terminated because of errors.
INFO: Time elapsed: 00:00:04.28
``` 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[profile.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22360264/profile.py)

title: [4.5] builtin_libjpeg_turbo=False not working
body:
### Tested versions

Godot Engine v4.5.stable.exherbo.876b29033

### System information

`Godot v4.5.stable (876b29033) - Exherbo #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon Sep 15 14:43:55 CEST 2025 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated Intel(R) UHD Graphics 770 (ADL-S GT1) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12500 (12 threads) - 15.27 GiB memory`

### Issue description

`builtin_libjpeg_turbo=False` produces a binary not linked against system libjpeg-turbo:

`ldd /usr/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/bin/godot | grep jpeg` returns nothing, while it works for other libraries like e.g. libpng:

```
ldd /usr/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/bin/godot | grep png
        libpng16.so.16 => /usr/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/lib/libpng16.so.16 (0x00007fbfc31aa000)
```
To be sure the bundled libraries are not used during build we also remove them, e.g. for libpng:

`rm -rf thirdparty/libpng`

So when adding libjpeg-turbo to that list `rm -rf thirdparty/libjpeg-turbo` the build fails with:

```
[...]
scons: *** [bin/obj/thirdparty/libjpeg-turbo/src/jcapistd_8.linuxbsd.editor.x86_64.o] Source `thirdparty/libjpeg-turbo/src/jcapistd.c' not found, needed by target `bin/obj/thirdparty/libjpeg-turbo/src/jcapistd_8.linuxbsd.editor.x86_64.o'.
```
also indicating that the bundled copy is still used.

Complete build log: [godot-4.5_build.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22362844/godot-4.5_build.log)

### Steps to reproduce

1. have libjpeg-turbo installed system-wide
2. remove from godot 4.5 sources `rm -rf thirdparty/libjpeg-turbo`
3. try to build with `builtin_libjpeg_turbo=False`

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

title: _get_configuration_warning only runs for scene root on save, and icon doesn't disappear when warnings clear up.
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in Godot 4.4.1
- Reproducible in Godot 4.5


### System information

Operating System: Linux Mint 21.3, Kernel: Linux 5.15.0-153-generic

### Issue description

There's something odd happening with the _get_configuration_warnings method. It doesn't update as you'd expect. Sometimes the icon stays alive, while there are no warnings left. Sometimes the icon doesn't show up, while there should be warnings.

- _get_configuration_warnings runs for root nodes of a scene:
    - when opening / switching scenes in the editor.
    - when saving a scene in the editor
- _get_configuration_warnings only runs for child nodes of a scene:
   - when opening / switching scenes in the editor.
   - when the child node is added to the scene.
   - it does NOT run when saving the root scene in the editor.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project
2. Create any type of Node
3. Attach this script to the root node.

```
@tool
extends Node

@export var show_warning: bool = true


func _get_configuration_warnings() -> PackedStringArray:
	print("Running _get_configuration_warnings in test_configuration_warnings.gd")
	var warnings := PackedStringArray()
	if show_warning:
		warnings.append("This is a configuration warning from test_configuration_warnings.gd")
	return warnings
```

4. Save the scene, and see the configuration warning pop up.
5. Uncheck the show_warning bool, save the scene, and see the configuration warning disappear.
6. Add a child node to the scene.
7. Add the same script to that child node.
8. See the configuration warning pop up.
9. Uncheck the show_warning bool, save the scene, and see that the configuration warning icon still shows.
10. Clicking the icon shows the print message in the console (proofing that _get_configuration_warning is still running, but no warning dialogue shows up, as the PackedStringArray is now empty.
11. Create a new scene. Switch to it.
12. Switch back to the old scene. The warning icon is now gone.
13. Check the show_warning bool again, and see how the warning icon does not pop up, nor does the error message print to the console.
14. Switch back to the new scene.
15. Switch back to the old scene.
16. Notice how the configuration warning icon is back again, and clicking it shows the error dialogue window.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test_conf-warnings_godot45.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22365224/test_conf-warnings_godot45.zip)
[test_conf_warnings_godot441.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22365223/test_conf_warnings_godot441.zip)

title: Unexpected Interaction between Sprite2D and SubViewport when using "Snap 2D Transforms to Pixel"
body: ### Tested versions v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] ### System information Windows 11 ### Issue description My scene consists of a SubViewport in a SubViewportContainer. Inside the SubViewport, I have some Sprite2Ds. These Sprite2Ds have 1x1 pixel sized textures and are scaled, e.g. to 16x16 or 64x16. When activating "Snap 2D Transforms to Pixel", these Sprite2Ds are not rendered at their actual location if their "centered" property is turned on. Instead, they are shifted by half their width/height. This becomes apparent when overlaying a CollisionShape2D. ### Steps to reproduce See above: Set up the scene as shown, have a 1x1 sized texture, add it to scaled Sprite2Ds inside the SubViewport and turn on "Snap 2D Transforms to Pixel". Turning off either "Snap 2D Transforms to Pixel" or the "centered" Property of the Sprite2D mitigates the effect. The issue also seems to not occur when using a sprite texture larger than 1x1 pixels. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [subviewport-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22366709/subviewport-test.zip)

author: JesusRoberto64

title: AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db doesn't work for non-Master buses
body:
### Tested versions

Exists: Godot 4.4, Godot 4.5

### System information

Godot version: 4.5 OS: MacOS (Apple M1 chip) Audio device: Built-in speakers/headphones

### Issue description

I'm experiencing an issue where AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db() doesn't actually change the volume for any audio buses other than "Master". The function executes without errors, but the volume remains unchanged.

Steps to reproduce:

Create a new audio bus (e.g., "Music") in Audio
Create a script that calls AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db() for this bus
Observe that the volume doesn't change, even though the function returns without errors
Code sample:

```gdscript

var music_bus_idx = AudioServer.get_bus_index("Music")
if music_bus_idx != -1:
    print("Before:", AudioServer.get_bus_volume_db(music_bus_idx))
    AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db(music_bus_idx, linear_to_db(0.5))  # Set to 50% volume
    print("After:", AudioServer.get_bus_volume_db(music_bus_idx))
    # Both print statements show the same value
```
Expected behavior:
The volume of the specified audio bus should change according to the value passed to set_bus_volume_db().

Actual behavior:
The volume remains unchanged, though the function doesn't report any errors. The issue occurs specifically on MacOS with M1 chip, but the "Master" bus works correctly.

Workaround:
Directly controlling the volume on individual AudioStreamPlayer nodes instead of using audio buses.

System information:

Godot version: 4.5
OS: MacOS (Apple M1 chip)
Audio device: Built-in speakers/headphones
Additional context:
I've verified that:

The audio buses are correctly created and named
No audio effects are applied to the buses
AudioStreamPlayer nodes are correctly assigned to the buses
The issue affects all non-Master buses
The problem appears to be in the audio server implementation for MacOS M1, specifically in how it handles volume changes for secondary audio buses.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a new audio bus (e.g., "Music") in Audio
Create a script that calls AudioServer.set_bus_volume_db() for this bus
Observe that the volume doesn't change, even though the function returns without errors

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: Jolt Physics - Impacted performance when placing ShapeCast3Ds in the same global position as bodies' collision shapes
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable.mono, 4.5.stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Max (Apple8) - Apple M2 Max (12 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Let's say I have 50 instances of a `StaticBody3D` scene that looks like this:
```
StaticBody3D
 └─ CollisionShape3D
```
where `StaticBody3D` is a `StaticBody3D`, and `CollisionShape3D` uses a `SphereShape3D` with a radius of 1 unit. `CollisionShape3D` is not transformed;

and 1 instance of a `ShapeCast3D` scene that looks like this:
```
ShapeCast3D
```
where `ShapeCast3D` uses a `SphereShape3D` with a radius of 5 units.

If I then place those 50 instances of `StaticBody3D`s and the `ShapeCast3D` instance in the same exact position (e.g., `Vector3.ZERO`) such that they overlap with one another, the fps will then plummet for some reason. But if the `ShapeCast3D` is translated even a little bit away from the `StaticBody3D` instances, the fps will return to normal.

Some observations:
- This issue seems to happen when using Jolt Physics only, since I can't reproduce it using the default physics engine.
- This issue only happens when the `StaticBody3D`s' `CollisionShape3D`'s global position (not the `StaticBody3D`s' global position) is the same as the `ShapeCast3D`'s global position.
- `CollisionShape3D`s placed as children under other `CollisionObject3D`-inherited classes can also lead to this issue.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download the minimal reproduction project. All scripts are written in GDScript, so it can be run without a .NET version of Godot.
2. Open `main.tscn`, run the project. This will spawn 50 instances of `static_body.tscn` under the node `BodyParent`, and 1 instance of `shape_cast.tscn` under the node `ShapeCastParent`.
3. Click on the `reset` button in the running project, this sets both `BodyParent` and `ShapeCastParent` to the same global position (`Vector3.ZERO`). Take note of the fps, it should be very low.
4. Then, click on the `translated` button, this translates `ShapeCastParent` slightly to `Vector3(0.1, 0, 0)` so that it won't share the same global position as `BodyParent`. Take note of the fps as well, this time it should be normal.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22370406/mrp.zip)

Godot 4.5 - iOS 26

### Issue description

Small Deploy with Remote Filesystem is not working on iOS, it prints to console the following log:

```
ERROR: Could not create directory: '/private/var/containers/Bundle/Application/A998D13C-9F82-492F-82F3-FF46214BB3CA/Test.app/remote_filesystem_files'.
at: make_dir_recursive (core/io/dir_access.cpp:180)

at: _store_file (core/io/remote_filesystem_client.cpp:99)

at: _store_cache_file (core/io/remote_filesystem_client.cpp:121)
ERROR: Error writing the remote filesystem file cache.
at: _synchronize_with_server (core/io/remote_filesystem_client.cpp:323)
Could not connect to remotefs: <IP>:6010.
````

### Steps to reproduce

- Open a project in Godot, enable `Small Deploy with Remote Filesystem`
- Export iOS Project, open in Xcode and add the launch argument `--remote-fs <IP>`
- Run the app on an iOS device 
- Give the app Local Network access permission when asked (or you can find it in Settings > Privacy & Security > Local Network)

author: FreedomEr-xst

title: Godot 4.5 FreeBSD build failure - depends on linuxisms
body:
### Tested versions

Built attempt of 4.5 branch 44c3f6a55a50dbbe884a668c522ad047768c0294
Branch 4.4 builds without issue

### System information

FreeBSD 15.0 amd64

### Issue description

Building Godot from source on FreeBSD 15.0 fails with the 4.5 branch. The first point of failure is the change introduced by 01fc9aee6c47aa1a1c787c46469fa18c21d06c66 that replaces the C header stdlib.h include directive with its C++ equivalent cstdlib. However this causes C code to fail to compile. The following patch enables the build to continue.

```patch
diff --git a/platform/linuxbsd/platform_config.h b/platform/linuxbsd/platform_config.h
index 377be301c1..3c58d7ecf5 100644
--- a/platform/linuxbsd/platform_config.h
+++ b/platform/linuxbsd/platform_config.h
@@ -35,7 +35,11 @@
 #endif
 
 #if defined(__FreeBSD__) || defined(__OpenBSD__) || defined(__NetBSD__)
+#ifdef __cplusplus
 #include <cstdlib> // alloca
+#else
+#include <stdlib.h>
+#endif
 // FreeBSD and OpenBSD use pthread_set_name_np, while other platforms,
 // include NetBSD, use pthread_setname_np. NetBSD's version however requires
 // a different format, we handle this directly in thread_posix.
```

Afterwards the build fails with

```
Compiling thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/SDL_evdev.c ...
Compiling thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/SDL_evdev_capabilities.c ...
In file included from thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/SDL_evdev.c:42:
thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/../../core/linux/SDL_evdev_capabilities.h:29:10: fatal error: 'linux/input.h' file not found
   29 | #include <linux/input.h>
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1 error generated.
scons: *** [bin/obj/thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/SDL_evdev.linuxbsd.editor.x86_64.llvm.anima.o] Error 1
In file included from thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/SDL_evdev_capabilities.c:24:
thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/SDL_evdev_capabilities.h:29:10: fatal error: 'linux/input.h' file not found
   29 | #include <linux/input.h>
      |          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
1 error generated.
scons: *** [bin/obj/thirdparty/sdl/core/linux/SDL_evdev_capabilities.linuxbsd.editor.x86_64.llvm.anima.o] Error 1
scons: building terminated because of errors.
```
Which is caused by the header not being found in the system includes, as it comes from the ports repository (devel/evdev-proto). It can be amended by this patch
```patch
diff --git a/drivers/sdl/SCsub b/drivers/sdl/SCsub
index dfd0807b7b..4a6f57c7f9 100644
--- a/drivers/sdl/SCsub
+++ b/drivers/sdl/SCsub
@@ -121,6 +121,7 @@ if env["builtin_sdl"]:
     if env["platform"] == "linuxbsd":
         # TODO: Check support for BSD systems.
         env_sdl.Append(CPPDEFINES=["SDL_PLATFORM_LINUX"])
+        env_sdl.Append(CPPPATH=["/usr/local/include"])
         thirdparty_sources += [
             "core/linux/SDL_dbus.c",
             "core/linux/SDL_evdev.c",
```
However there is another build failure soon after:
```
thirdparty/sdl/thread/pthread/SDL_systhread.c:248:34: error: use of undeclared identifier 'SYS_gettid'
  248 |         pid_t linuxTid = syscall(SYS_gettid);
      |                                  ^
1 error generated.
```
Which is another linuxism. SDL does have a SDL_PLATFORM_FREEBSD define that skips this Linux-specific code but with that defined instead of SDL_PLATFORM_LINUX it then fails building with:
```
drivers/sdl/SDL_build_config_private.h:133:2: error: "No SDL build config was found for this platform. Setup one before compiling the engine."
  133 | #error "No SDL build config was found for this platform. Setup one before compiling the engine."  
```
A quick and dirty edit of the define check in `SDL_systhread.c` to skip this problem code, allows the build to complete.
```patch
--- a/thirdparty/sdl/thread/pthread/SDL_systhread.c
+++ b/thirdparty/sdl/thread/pthread/SDL_systhread.c
@@ -243,7 +243,7 @@ bool SDL_SYS_SetThreadPriority(SDL_ThreadPriority priority)
         policy = pri_policy;
     }
 
-#ifdef SDL_PLATFORM_LINUX
+#ifdef SDL_PLATFORM_NOLINUX
     {
         pid_t linuxTid = syscall(SYS_gettid);
         return SDL_SetLinuxThreadPriorityAndPolicy(linuxTid, priority, policy);
```
And those are all the changes you need to build Godot 4.5 on FreeBSD 15.0.

### Steps to reproduce

```
scons target=editor use_llvm=yes linker=mold
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Not relevant

title: Changing text trough other node global node function call doesn't deletes old text
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] ,v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]
- Do not reproducible in: i don't have time for checking this one

### System information

Arch Linux, Gnome DE - v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] - Vulkan 1.4.321 (Forward+) - 11th Gen Intel i3-1115G4 (4) @ 4.100GHz 

### Issue description

If you try to change the text of the Label node through a custom function by calling this function in another node, making the node where this function is in another node global, then the old text will remain along with the new one.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a main control node scene with a button as a child
2. Make another control scene but with label in it(also make sure that there is text in label)
3. Make that one scene with label global
4. Put scene with label in scene with button
5. Create script attached to the scene with label
6. In that script write code with custom function that changes text property of label to something else
7. Create script attached to the button
8. Connect signal "pressed" to custom function that calls custom function in scene with label
9. Now when you press button the new text appear on top of label, while old text is still here

title: Invalid parameters on audio effect silences audio completely instead of throwing errors
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducable on Windows 11, Godot 4.4.1 and 4.5

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7659) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-13450HX (16 threads) - 23.71 GiB memory

### Issue description

Recently spent way to much time tracking down a bug because it lacked any errors.

Some place in my code I had accidentally set an AudioEffectReverb's paremeter room_size to be NaN.
Each time reverb was to be applied it instead silenced every sound in the game for its duration, before then returning all to normal.

Would be convenient if it instead threw an error, indicating that the room_size value was invalid. It seems dangerous that it just fails silently.

### Steps to reproduce

1) Create project with an audio player and any stream.
2) Alternatively, also add an autoplaying background track.
3) At Audio tab add a reverb effect
4) In code, set reverb's room_size to a NaN value and play the AudioPlayer.
5) Observe as the audio mutes itself (both the played player as well as the background track) with no error indicating what's wrong.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

no
author: SteinMakesGames

title: [4.5] Cannot edit values of Resources of Instanced Scenes until making it unique
body:
### Tested versions

Tested on v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]
Was behaving differently in v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT (RADV NAVI22) - AMD Ryzen 9 7950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

When I instance a new scene of an already existing object, and this object has an exported Resource that is not filled, when I fill this variable with a resource, I cannot edit any of the values of the resource until I explicitly make it unique.

For example, I have a Room, and I add a Box to it. 
The Box needs to have a Resource (exported variable), but the content can be one of many different Resources, hence it is not set to be one specific one.
In the Room scene that I have added the Box to, I am now adding a Resource (of type Money).

In the previous version of Godot (4.4), I could immediately edit the values of the Money resource.
In 4.5, I fist have to make the Money resource unique.

If the new behavior is intended, it is, in my opinion, just an additional step with no clear benefit. That said, I could not see anything specifically talking about this behavior in the Changelog for 4.5

### Steps to reproduce

- Have a scene (Room)
- add an instanced scene (Box) to Room, that can hold a resource, with no resource being set inside of the Box scene itself.
- Add a Resource to the Box node inside of the Room scene
- you cannot edit any of the values of the added resource until you make the resource unique.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[resource-4.5.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22385974/resource-4.5.zip)

title: Typed Array/Dictionary incompatible with ternary assignment
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS 24.04 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon 760M (radeonsi, phoenix, LLVM 20.1.2, DRM 3.57, 6.8.0-79-generic) - AMD Ryzen 5 7640U w/ Radeon 760M Graphics (12 threads) - 13.45 GiB memory

### Issue description

You cannot define types of Arrays/Dictionaries if they are assigned with a ternary expression.
If you just define an Array/Dictionary without value, then assign a value in each conditional branch, it works.
Shouldn't these methods be equal?

The error is only shown at runtime, it expects just a plain Array/Dictionary:
`Trying to assign a dictionary of type "Dictionary" to a variable of type "Dictionary[String, int]".`

Edit: If you type cast both values, it works with a typed Array/Dictionary but that seems very unnecessary.

### Steps to reproduce

```GDScript
var condition: bool = true

# does not work, errors at runtime
# but does work with untyped Dictionary
var data_ternary: Dictionary[String, int] = {
	old = 0,
	new = 1,
} if condition else {
	old = 8,
	new = 9,
}

# does work
var data: Dictionary[String, int] = {}

if condition:
	data.old = 0
	data.new = 1
else:
	data.old = 8
	data.new = 9
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A
author: notroid5

title: CompositorEffect not getting accurate stencil buffer unless I switch effect_callback_type off of Post-Transparent, then back
body: ### Tested versions - Reproduces on 4.5-stable ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sonoma (14.7.8) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Max (Apple9) - Apple M3 Max (16 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory ### Issue description I have a CompositorEffect that draws outlines based on values in the stencil buffer. Even though the stencil values appear to be correct (checking in a debug quad with a stencil-read shader), the outlines are not drawn when the process starts. Here's where it gets weird, if I switch the CompositorEffect callback type from Post-Transparent, to Pre-Transparent, then back to Post-Transparent, it begins to work. In the attached video, you'll see this, along with trying to just disable/enable the CompositorEffect to get it to work. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cef35052-b9bb-4785-906b-8a1d3b12ce70 One additional note, if I switch the orange StandardMaterial in the MRP to write the stencil itself, the outlines work immediately. It looks like it may be related to using a dumb stencil-write ShaderMaterial, then a StandardMaterial as the next_pass. Dumb shader for writing to stencil buffer: ```gdshader shader_type spatial; render_mode blend_mix; stencil_mode write, compare_always, 1; void fragment() { ALPHA = 0.0; } ``` ### Steps to reproduce * Start the scene in the MRP * Note that there are no yellow outlines * Toggle the DebugStencilQuad to see where the outlines should be drawn * In the Camera3D, under Compositor, in the first Compositor, `StencilBasedOutine...` change "Enabled" to false, then true * Nothing will change on screen * In the Compositor settings "Effect Callback Type" from "Post Transparent" to "Pre Transparent" * Nothing will change on screen * Change the "Effect Callback Type" back to "Post Transparent" * Outlines will now be shown on screen Location of CompositorEffect settings because they're deep in there: . ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [next_pass-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22393349/next_pass-issue.zip)

title: RichTextLabel text property not changing with Line Separation being set on Theme or Theme Override.
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5.1 stable [Linux Desktop], 4.5 stable [Linux and Windows Desktop], and 4.5 stable [Web Editor]
Not tested: Versions prior to 4.4.1 (But memory serves Line Separation worked without issues for me since messing with Themes. And I started using Godot at version 3)
Not reproducible: 4.4.1 stable [Linux Desktop]

### System information

Linux Mint+Web Browser, Godot 4.5 (stable) - Vulkan (forward)/Compatibility on Web Browser - AMD 6900 XT dedicated GPU

### Issue description

At first I updated from 4.4.1 stable which has working Line Separation on Themes to 4.5 stable. I noticed my game no longer had this property working. This was on desktop Linux. I tried to set it on Theme Override as well with no results. Lines were not any less or more separated when value was adjusted.

I then created a new project on the browser to rule out it just being a Linux thing or my own project converting wonky. The issue persisted even on a brand new project. This behavior is observed in both the Editor and in runtime for the game.

### Steps to reproduce

Create or open an existing project -> Create a Node2d or Control node (Issue happens as child of both) -> Create RichTextLabel as child of Node2d or Control node -> Create a Theme, or use Theme Override (Issue happens with both values) -> Set "Line Separation" to any value other than default (0).

Last step, write any two lines of text into the "Text" field of the RichTextLabel. Ctrl+S to save. You will see in Editor the spacing doesn't change like in 4.4.1. Same happens if you go into runtime.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[line-separation-example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22393876/line-separation-example.zip)
author: jplover24

title: RigidBody2D does not render position change via Rotation of Parent (Regression)
body: ### Tested versions Tested with Godot 4.5 and 4.41. In 4.5 the problem is reproducible. ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 22621) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7270) - AMD Ryzen 9 5950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 127.9 GiB memory ### Issue description When parenting a RigidBody2D to another node and adding an position offset, the parent's rotation does not change the RigidBody2D's position visually. The RigidBody2D's "Can Sleep" is disabled, and it's sleeping status is false. Checking the remote parameters in the inspector shows the RigidBody2D's position being changed. Might be connected to sleeping. Only happening in 4.5 compared to 4.4.1. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/920b78c9-ddbc-4a4a-b62d-61b5481c60a6 ### Steps to reproduce 1. Setup new Node2D scene. 2. Add Node2D as child, called "Rotator". 3. Attach new script to Rotator: `func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void: rotation = rotation+0.4 * delta ` 4. Add RigidBody2D as child of Rotator. 5. Add Sprite2D and CollisionShape2D to RigidBody2D. 6. Disable "Can Sleep" in RigidBody2D 7. Change RigidBody2D's position.x to +200 (or any offset) 8. In editor, change Rotator's rotation and validate RigidBody2D's global_position changing as expected. 9. Start scene 10. RigidBody2D should not be moving. 11. Checking RigidBody2D's remote parameters in the inspector should show the position changing, but the Node will not update visually. When leaving "Can Sleep" in RigidBody2D enabled, there is a short latency before body is moved. Might be an issue with sleeping? ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [rotation-bug-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22393922/rotation-bug-test.zip)
author: abrossimow

title: Pen Tablet pressure not correctly detected in HTML
body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.4.1, 4.5 ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 22621) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12500H (16 threads) - 15.69 GiB memory ### Issue description Pen tablet pressure is recognized correctly under an InputEventMouseMotion event in _input, if running on Windows. However, if running a HTML deploy for the browser, two events actually fire and the second event is always 0, making the final pressure value at the end of a frame be 0, essentially not detecting pressure on browser. Windows behavior, pressure being read correctly: Browser behavior, pressure is 0.0 every other event in each frame: I thought this could be my 120hz monitor being funny but limiting the fps to 30 or even 10 doesn't remove the issue. Another observation I don't know if should be a separate bug or what: > Mouse pressure with a normal mouse is 0.5 on browser but 1.0 on windows, which is inconsistent behavior and, as it's impossible to tell if the input is from a mouse or tablet, almost impossible to work around. I could get my pen tablet to print exactly 0.5 pressure so I can't just == compare that. ### Steps to reproduce make a html browser export template create an _input(event) function if event is InputEventMouseMotion, save the pressure to a variable use the variable somewhere after all inputs have been processed (say, in _process) check that value in a browser deploy ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [pen-pressure-browser.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22394874/pen-pressure-browser.zip) EDIT: Grammar
author: natanmaia95

title: [4.6 dev] Godot editor sometimes crashes on entry and exit on macOS
body:
### Tested versions

Current master / 4.6 dev (8b4b93a82e13cb1b7ea5fa28b39163a6311a0bb3)

### System information

macOS 15.3

### Issue description

Currently, Godot sometimes crashes either on entry or exit. I'm not sure what caused this, but it must be one of the recent merges.

After looking at backtraces (below), I think it's because functions are getting called with through `thread_callback` with `EditorExportPlatformAppleEmbedded *` parameters, which are getting destructed in another thread at the same time.

#### Entry crash backtrace

```
libc++abi: Pure virtual function called!
Process 67512 stopped
* thread #33, stop reason = signal SIGABRT
    frame #0: 0x000000019b3f7720 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 8
libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill:
->  0x19b3f7720 <+8>:  b.lo   0x19b3f7740    ; <+40>
    0x19b3f7724 <+12>: pacibsp
    0x19b3f7728 <+16>: stp    x29, x30, [sp, #-0x10]!
    0x19b3f772c <+20>: mov    x29, sp
Target 0: (godot.macos.editor.arm64.master) stopped.
(lldb) bt
* thread #33, stop reason = signal SIGABRT
  * frame #0: 0x000000019b3f7720 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 8
    frame #1: 0x000000019b42ff70 libsystem_pthread.dylib`pthread_kill + 288
    frame #2: 0x000000019b33c908 libsystem_c.dylib`abort + 128
    frame #3: 0x000000019b3e644c libc++abi.dylib`abort_message + 132
    frame #4: 0x000000019b3e5d24 libc++abi.dylib`__cxa_pure_virtual + 24
    frame #5: 0x00000001021d5d74 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`EditorExportPlatformAppleEmbedded::_check_for_changes_poll_thread(ud=0x00000001262712e0) at editor_export_platform_apple_embedded.cpp:2408:29 [opt]
    frame #6: 0x0000000101a6ceb4 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`Thread::thread_callback(p_data=0x0000600005dedf60) at thread_apple.cpp:55:2 [opt]
    frame #7: 0x000000019b4302e4 libsystem_pthread.dylib`_pthread_start + 136
```

#### Exit crash backtrace
```
* thread #23, stop reason = signal SIGABRT
    frame #0: 0x000000019b3f7720 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 8
libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill:
->  0x19b3f7720 <+8>:  b.lo   0x19b3f7740    ; <+40>
    0x19b3f7724 <+12>: pacibsp
    0x19b3f7728 <+16>: stp    x29, x30, [sp, #-0x10]!
    0x19b3f772c <+20>: mov    x29, sp
Target 0: (godot.macos.editor.arm64.master) stopped.
(lldb) bt
* thread #23, stop reason = signal SIGABRT
  * frame #0: 0x000000019b3f7720 libsystem_kernel.dylib`__pthread_kill + 8
    frame #1: 0x000000019b42ff70 libsystem_pthread.dylib`pthread_kill + 288
    frame #2: 0x000000019b33c908 libsystem_c.dylib`abort + 128
    frame #3: 0x000000019b3e644c libc++abi.dylib`abort_message + 132
    frame #4: 0x000000019b3e5d24 libc++abi.dylib`__cxa_pure_virtual + 24
    frame #5: 0x00000001021d6054 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`EditorExportPlatformAppleEmbedded::_check_for_changes_poll_thread(ud=0x0000000120f1fb70) at editor_export_platform_apple_embedded.cpp:2440:47 [opt]
    frame #6: 0x0000000101a6ceb4 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`Thread::thread_callback(p_data=0x0000600001b78fd0) at thread_apple.cpp:55:2 [opt]
    frame #7: 0x000000019b4302e4 libsystem_pthread.dylib`_pthread_start + 136
```

I looked at the main thread during an exit crash, and got the following backtrace:

```
  * frame #0: 0x000000019b3f0cac libsystem_kernel.dylib`__ulock_wait + 8
    frame #1: 0x000000019b4327d4 libsystem_pthread.dylib`_pthread_join + 612
    frame #2: 0x0000000101a6d194 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`Thread::wait_to_finish(this=0x0000000120f1fdc0) at thread_apple.cpp:112:12 [opt]
    frame #3: 0x00000001021dcf9c godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`EditorExportPlatformAppleEmbedded::~EditorExportPlatformAppleEmbedded(this=0x0000000120f1fb70) at editor_export_platform_apple_embedded.cpp:2899:28 [opt]
    frame #4: 0x00000001003afa90 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`CowData<Ref<ScriptBacktrace>>::_unref() [inlined] void memdelete<RefCounted>(p_class=0x0000000120f1fb70) at memory.h:139:13 [opt]
    frame #5: 0x00000001003afa74 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`CowData<Ref<ScriptBacktrace>>::_unref() [inlined] Ref<ScriptBacktrace>::unref(this=<unavailable>) at ref_counted.h:206:5 [opt]
    frame #6: 0x00000001003afa60 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`CowData<Ref<ScriptBacktrace>>::_unref() [inlined] Ref<ScriptBacktrace>::~Ref(this=<unavailable>) at ref_counted.h:220:3 [opt]
    frame #7: 0x00000001003afa60 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`CowData<Ref<ScriptBacktrace>>::_unref() [inlined] Ref<ScriptBacktrace>::~Ref(this=<unavailable>) at ref_counted.h:219:9 [opt]
    frame #8: 0x00000001003afa60 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`CowData<Ref<ScriptBacktrace>>::_unref(this=<unavailable>) at cowdata.h:260:17 [opt]
    frame #9: 0x000000010218a974 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`EditorExport::~EditorExport() [inlined] CowData<Ref<EditorExportPlatform>>::~CowData(this=<unavailable>) at cowdata.h:224:30 [opt]
    frame #10: 0x000000010218a970 godot.macos.editor.arm64.master`EditorExport::~EditorExport() [inlined] CowData<Ref<EditorExportPlatform>>::~CowData(this=<unavailable>) at cowdata.h:224:28 [opt]
```

author: Ivorforce

title: duplicate() can break references when referenced node name contains @ symbol
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M3 Pro (Apple9) - Apple M3 Pro (12 threads) - 18.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Duplicating a node tree that contains a node that references another node inside the same tree (e.g. via an exported var) when the referenced node name contains an @ symbol breaks that link in the duplicated node.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a parent node with 2 children, add a reference from one child to the other (e.g. via an exported var)
2. Ensure referenced child node uses default name (contains @ symbol)
3. duplicate() the parent node
4. The duplicate child node that referenced the other child won't reference anything

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp-lost-reference-on-duplicate.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22395277/mrp-lost-reference-on-duplicate.zip)

title: If the game window is embedded on macOS, disabling accumulated input causes just_pressed and just_released functions to never return true
body:
### Tested versions

Tested on:
- v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]
- v4.5.stable.mono.official [876b29033]

Tested with both GDScript and C# scripts; uploaded a GDScript repro project

(Did not test earlier versions, as macOS embedded play is new to 4.5)

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable.mono - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 Max (Apple7) - Apple M1 Max (10 threads) - 64.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

I am running Godot 4.5 on macOS, with the game window embedded (i.e. "Embed Game on Next Play" is checked).

If I have a script call `Input.set_use_accumulated_input(false)`, then `Input.is_action_just_pressed(...)` and `Input.is_action_just_released(...)` will always return false. However, `Input.is_action_pressed(...)` does seem to work correctly.

Removing the call to `Input.set_use_accumulated_input(false)` "fixes" the issue. Additionally, unchecking "Embed Game on Next Play" will also cause the issue to go away.

### Steps to reproduce

(N.B. I've attached a minimal repro project that includes a scene with the node/script mentioned)

1. Open a project in Godot 4.5 on macOS
2. In the "Game" tab, under the embedding options (far right "snowman" menu with three dots), ensure that "Embed Game on Next Play" is checked
3. Run the game, with the following script attached to a node in the scene:
```gdscript
extends Node

func _init() -> void:
	Input.set_use_accumulated_input(false)

func _process(delta: float):
	if Input.is_action_just_pressed("ui_accept"):
		print("Accept just pressed")
	
	if Input.is_action_pressed("ui_accept"):
		print("Accept is pressed")
		
	if Input.is_action_just_released("ui_accept"):
		print("Accept just released")
```
4. Press the Enter key on your keyboard

**Observed Behavior:**
The Output log looks like the following:
```
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
```

**Expected Behavior:**
The log should instead look like the following:
```
Accept just pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept is pressed
Accept just released
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[input_issue_demo_2025-09-17_17-09-59.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22396349/input_issue_demo_2025-09-17_17-09-59.zip)
author: ScarlettHoefler

This happens incrementally every time the window is closed and reopened; the screenshots below are each after one close/reopen from the previous screenshot. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open the Project Settings window 2. Close the Project Settings window 3. Reopen the Project Settings window ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: get_tree().root.find_children always returns nothing
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]
- Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.fedora [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

Fedora Linux 41 - Wayland Gnome 47 - Linux 6.16.7-100.fc41.x86_64- Reproducible in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] - Vulkan (Forward+) - AMD Radeon™ RX 6600 - AMD Ryzen™ 5 5600 × 12 - 16.0 GiB RAM

### Issue description

`get_tree().root.find_children("*")` always returns an empty array. This also occurs regardless of the node name passed or the node type. Waiting for the root node to be ready does not change the behavior of this bug. 

Note: `find_children()` is recursive by default, so no need to specify it.

### Steps to reproduce

1) Create a new project
2) Add a root node of any type, give it any name
3) Give that root node children of any types and with any names (optional)
4) On any node, attach a new script that runs get_tree().root.find_children("*") when the root is ready
5) Expect all nodes of the scene tree to be returned, but get an empty array


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

This bug appears in every project, but here's an MRP.

[test-4.5.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22398608/test-4.5.zip)
author: RolandMarchand

author: AaronRecord

title: Save All Scenes shortcut frequently does not work
body:
### Tested versions

Found in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], not present in 4.4 official. I consider this a UX side regression.

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7688) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900KF CPU @ 3.60GHz (16 threads) - 31.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

There are a number of primary symptoms in the 4.5 update affecting saving within the editor. Most of these stem to a change to the "save all scenes" shortcut.

1. Always: Shortcuts relating to saving scenes, including "save scene" and "save all scenes" will not trigger.
2. Rarely: The (*) in the window title will persist even after all scripts and scenes have been saved. Cause unknown.
3. Possibly: Many saving operations no longer cause a progress bar to appear. There is no feedback to whether or not a save has occurred.

I want to focus this issue on point number 1, as it is the root cause in why the editor feels different now. And it's preventing me from assessing if 2 and 3 are *actually* different now, or if its just a symptom of 1.

**Apparent behavior in 4.5**

* The "save all scenes" shortcut now requires the editor to be in a "scene editing" state to be able to trigger.
* This requires focusing the Scene dock, or interacting with a Scene in the Main view.
* This shortcut **sometimes does not work** even when the Main view is displaying a 2D/3D editor.
* It appears to only save scenes now. (Perhaps, due to it not saving all resources anymore, that is why the (*) is not clearing?)

**Apparent behavior in 4.4**

* The "save all scenes" shortcut would execute regardless of the open view.
* It would execute regardless of if any scenes actually need saving. Defined by the saving progress bar popup appearing for a split second, even when nothing was saved.
* It would function in conjunction with the script editor. Saving all open scripts, and saving all open scenes, and saving all edited resources in the inspector history.

**Attempted Remedies**
* Rebinding "save all scenes" to ctrl+s from its default (I have done this in every Godot version). This does not affect the behavior.
* Removing conflicting shortcuts, so that only this one shortcut is triggered by ctrl+s. This does not affect the behavior.

**Proposed fix**
Either, once again make she shortcut independent of the current editor focus.
Or, allow setting shortcuts as "global" so individual shortcuts can ignore the editor focus.
Or, add a "save absolutely everything everywhere" shortcut! :D

### Steps to reproduce

1. Edit a scene
2. Move to the script editor
3. Use the "save all scenes" shortcut (not from the menu, but actually hit the shortcut)
4. Nothing happens | Previous this would save all scripts, scenes, and resources.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

-

title: Invalid Cast Exception when Duplicating a node with C# script that has [Export] for another node with C# script
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable.mono - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8088) - AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

When duplicating a node with C# script that has an `[Export]` of another node with C# script, there is an issue with casting and `Invalid Cast Exception` is thrown.
It's very easy to reproduce and I can even show the whole code here.

First we have the root of the project that will duplicate a node.
```cs
public partial class Root : Node
{
    [Export] private NodeToDuplicate _nodeToDuplicate;

    public override void _Ready()
    {
        var e = _nodeToDuplicate.Duplicate();
    }
}
```

Then we have a node with a C# script (I only tested on Control, not sure about other nodes) that has an `[Export]` with another C# script Control.
```cs
public partial class NodeToDuplicate : Control
{
    [Export] private NotCastableChild _notCastableChild;
}
```

And the final node that has an issue being casted.
```cs
public partial class NotCastableChild : Control;
```

It's important that it's only an issue when accessing the node through `[Export]`. It's possible to find it with `GetNode` and cast it later without issues.

The exact error is this:
```
E 0:00:01:475   VariantUtils.generic.cs:388 @ T Godot.NativeInterop.VariantUtils.ConvertTo<T>(Godot.NativeInterop.godot_variant&): System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object of type 'Godot.Control' to type 'NotCastableChild'.
  <C# Error>    System.InvalidCastException
  <C# Source>   /root/godot/modules/mono/glue/GodotSharp/GodotSharp/Core/NativeInterop/VariantUtils.generic.cs:388 @ T Godot.NativeInterop.VariantUtils.ConvertTo<T>(Godot.NativeInterop.godot_variant&)
  <Stack Trace> VariantUtils.generic.cs:388 @ T Godot.NativeInterop.VariantUtils.ConvertTo<T>(Godot.NativeInterop.godot_variant&)
                NodeToDuplicate_ScriptProperties.generated.cs:21 @ bool NodeToDuplicate.SetGodotClassPropertyValue(Godot.NativeInterop.godot_string_name&, Godot.NativeInterop.godot_variant&)
                CSharpInstanceBridge.cs:57 @ Godot.NativeInterop.godot_bool Godot.Bridge.CSharpInstanceBridge.Set(nint, Godot.NativeInterop.godot_string_name*, Godot.NativeInterop.godot_variant*)
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run the scene `main.tscn`
2. See that there is an error in the Debugger

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[invalid-cast-exception.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22412407/invalid-cast-exception.zip)

title: Compatibility HDR 2D: glow darkens the scene when `white > 1.0`
body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] and v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] - Possibly reproducible in Godot 4.3, but I'm having a hard time getting it to internally render values above 1.0 before tonemapping is applied ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8108) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-13700K (24 threads) ### Issue description When using a tonemapper such as Reinhard with a `white` parameter larger than `1.0`, enabling glow may darken the scene with HDR 2D enabled in the Compatibility renderer. This is because the glow effect assumes that `1.0` is the brightest scene value that won't be clipped, but when using a tonemapper with a `white` greater than `1.0`, a scene value larger than `1.0` may be mixed with glow. | Glow disabled | Glow enabled | --|-- || ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create a project with the Compatibility renderer 2. Enable HDR 2D 3. Create a 3D scene with an unshaded object that has an albedo that is brighter than `1.0` 4. Add a `WorldEnvironment` and set its tonemapper to Reinhard 5. Change the tonemapper `white` parameter to `1.0` 6. Enable glow ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [glow-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22413976/glow-test.zip)

title: GDScript Editor auto-completion suggestions does not update after erasing trailing dot access
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.stable, 3.6.1.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Linux Mint 22.2 (Zara) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (TGL GT2) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-1135G7 @ 2.40GHz (8 threads) - 7.40 GiB memory

### Issue description

When typing an expression in the Editor that involves accessing the property of an object, erasing the property when its ~4 characters long seems to incorrectly continue to show auto-complete suggestions for the previously accessed property. Example: ```get_tree().root```

*Expected*: ```get_tree().``` suggestions should show SceneTree properties after deleting the trailing ```root.```.
*Actual*: ```get_tree().``` suggestions still show Window related properties even after erasing the trailing ```root.```.

Additional Info:
- I'm using the built-in Script Editor with no plugins or extentions.
- 100% reliability of reproduction
- Attached: Demo of described issue in action

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b92352aa-88a8-4606-b728-3e6a6ce03c77)

### Steps to reproduce

- Open any GDScript file.
- Write an expression that accesses another property with a short name (example: ```get_tree().root```). Auto-completion shows Window properties/methods as intended.
- Erase the property after the dot operator (example: remove 'root' from ```get_tree().root```, leaving ```get_tree().```) Using either the Backspace Key (spamming or holding down), or selecting the text using Shift + Arrows, then Backspace. Auto-completion still shows Window properties/methods even tho a SceneTree is now being accessed.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: Godot 4.5 NavigationRegion2D/NavigationAgent2D Path finding doesn't work anymore
body: ### Tested versions reproduceable in 4.5 NOT reproduceable in 4.4.1 (it's working there) ### System information Windows 11 godot-4.5.stable ### Issue description Since the Update of Godot 4.5 the path finding in a NavigationRegion2D using a NavigationAgent2D doesn't work anymore (in Godot 4.4.1 it worked just seamlessly). Now when i bake the NavMesh in Godot for my NavigationRegion2D i get the following error: ` ERROR: Navigation region synchronization error. More than 2 edges tried to occupy the same map rasterization space. This is a logical error in the navigation mesh caused by overlap or too densely placed edges.` Now i have already seen a similar bug report from a 4.5 Beta i guess and someone said, that they changed the order of some checks in the 2D Navigation Code so i am aware that these order changes took place in order to achieve the independet 2D Navigation (which now isn't dependent from the 3D Navigation anymore). But still it would be really good to update the 2D Navigation Code so that it is working just the same as before in Godot 4.4.1 while still keeping it independent from the 3D Navigation. Since Google blocks all app/game updates which are not using 16KB page size starting with November 1st, it would be extremely important to address and fix this issue as soon as possible, since all developers that have issues with 4.5 will not be able to update their app/game anymore.. I already asked the Godot Forum for help and people told me the path finding should just work without changing anything at all, which it didn't, so these people told me it must be a bug and i should report it (even thought someone else already did it before in the Beta -> but it didn't get fixed) I hope you guys understand the importance of this issue.. I also attached a Screenshot of my NavMesh so you can see there are no overlapps or anything like that. I have seen people with simple Path findings that work, but i think on more complex NavigationRegion2D's the issue is almost guaranteed to happen. I think the Demo Program does actually work so i would not take it as reference. Here is also a video showcasing the exact problem: https://youtu.be/yWkHaDc3YN8 BR ### Steps to reproduce - Open the attached project with Godot 4.4.1 - Run the project - The red square goes directly to the target point - Open the attached project with Godot 4.5 (migration) - Run the project - The red square is stucked ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [bug-navigation.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22423757/bug-navigation.zip)

title: Casting error with children of `NetSocket` (no `GDSOFTCLASS`)
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 2753d33 (current `master`)

### System information

MacOS Sequoia Version 15.6.1 (24G90)

### Issue description

If a developer tries to directly pass in a `Ref<ChildNetSocket>` argument to a function expecting `Ref<NetSocket>`  where `ChildNetSocket` inherits from `NetSocket`, then after bc9d0c78350 was merged, the below error (or similar) appears:

```
Compiling tests/test_main.cpp ...
In file included from ./core/variant/binder_common.h:34,
                 from ./core/object/method_bind.h:33,
                 from ./core/object/class_db.h:33,
                 from ./core/object/ref_counted.h:33,
                 from ./core/io/dir_access.h:33,
                 from tests/test_main.cpp:34:
./core/object/object.h: In instantiation of 'static T* Object::cast_to(Object*) [with T = NetSocket]':
./core/object/ref_counted.h:145:40:   required from 'void Ref<T>::operator=(const Ref<T_Other>&) [with T_Other = TestStreamPeerTCP::MockNetSocket; T = NetSocket]'
  145 |                 ref_pointer<false>(Object::cast_to<T>(p_from.ptr()));
      |                                    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./core/object/ref_counted.h:182:18:   required from 'Ref<T>::Ref(const Ref<T_Other>&) [with T_Other = TestStreamPeerTCP::MockNetSocket; T = NetSocket]'
  182 |                 this->operator=(p_from);
      |                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~
./tests/core/io/test_stream_peer_tcp.h:192:21:   required from here
  192 |         spt->accept_socket(ns, peer_ip, peer_port);
      |                            ^~
./core/object/object.h:799:36: error: static assertion failed: T must use GDCLASS or GDSOFTCLASS
  799 |                 static_assert(std::is_same_v<std::decay_t<T>, typename T::self_type>, "T must use GDCLASS or GDSOFTCLASS");
      |                               ~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
./core/object/object.h:799:36: note: 'std::is_same_v<NetSocket, RefCounted>' evaluates to false
scons: *** [bin/obj/tests/test_main.linuxbsd.editor.x86_64.o] Error 1
scons: building terminated because of errors.
```

(thanks for the above, @Calinou )

The error surfaced during the development of this testing PR #102064, within `StreamPeerTCP::accept_socket()`, and was fixed by adding `GDSOFTCLASS(NetSocket, RefCounted);` to `NetSocket`, in file `core/io/net_socket.h`.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Pull the branch from my fork in #102064 
2. remove the line containing `GDSOFTCLASS` from `NetSocket` in `core/io/net_socket.h`
3. build including tests

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A - This is not an editor bug.

title: Visible Ratio on RichTextLabel doesn't update size dynamically in Godot 4.5
body:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.5

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

In Godot 4.5, changing visible_ratio in a RichTextLabel with fit_content=true does not dynamically adjust the node's size (neither minimum_size nor the rendered size), unlike Godot 4.4.1, where the box resized in real-time according to the visible text.

This is noticeable when a texture appears expanded to the final size of the text, even before the text has been fully displayed.

Here you have a couple of version comparisons.

Godot 4.4.1
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/87a924f0-8585-4684-8d0d-554c8850960c

Godot 4.5
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/68ab328a-077f-456d-ba1e-43d8ef008fe1

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a RichTextLabel with fit_content enabled.

2. Assign multi-line text.

3. Use a tween or script to change visible_ratio from 0.0 to 1.0.

4. Observe that the size does not adjust to the visible text (it remains the total content size).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I’m sharing a test project that includes the features where this bug is reproduced. As you will notice, when manually changing the visible_ratio, the text box consistently retains the final size of the RichTextLabel, as it does not adjust dynamically.

[test-ui.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22433650/test-ui.zip)
author: KickCreator

title: TextureStorage::texture_debug_usage and MeshStorage::mesh_debug_usage are not thread-safe
body:
### Tested versions

- current master @ 3c9d03b8755968ae305b8cfebe886c56402cb044

### System information

macOS Sequoia (15.6.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M4 Max (Apple9) - Apple M4 Max (16 threads)

### Issue description

texture_debug_usage and mesh_debug_usage are not thread-safe. While `get_owned_list()` is thread-safe, iterating through the list is not. The mutex is not held while iterating through the list, which means that textures/meshes can be freed during iteration, which can lead to a segfault when de-referencing them while populating the info objects.

This can be likely be fixed by just locking the mutex before iterating through the list.

### Steps to reproduce

1. constantly call either function on a task thread while loading and unloading scenes on the main thread
2. Eventually, you'll get a segfault.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: Android: CameraServer fails with "Expected Image data size" error on YUV_420_888 stream
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in:
- Godot Engine v4.5.stable.official.876b29033

I have not tested earlier or later versions. Given the nature of the bug, it appears to be a hardware-specific issue related to the handling of YUV camera streams, which may have been present in other versions as well.

### System information

OS: Android 13   -   Device: Oppo CPH2565   -   GPU: Qualcomm Adreno (TM) 610   -   Godot Version: v4.5.stable.official.876b29033   -   Rendering Backend: OpenGL ES 3.2 (Compatibility)

### Issue description

Activating the `CameraServer` on certain Android devices results in a **black screen** being displayed instead of the camera feed, while the debugger is **continuously spammed** with C++ errors.

This occurs on devices where the camera's only available output format is `YUV_420_888`. The errors (`Expected Image data size... got... bytes instead`) strongly indicate that Godot is not correctly handling memory stride/padding from the camera driver's raw YUV data stream.

The expected behavior is that the camera feed should be displayed correctly without errors.

**Error Log:**
```cpp
E 0:00:06:113   _texture_set_data: Condition "texture->format != p_image->get_format()" is true.
  <C++ Source>  drivers/gles3/storage/texture_storage.cpp:1551 @ _texture_set_data()
E 0:00:06:173   initialize_data: Expected Image data size of 2400x1080x1 (Red8 without mipmaps) = 2592000 bytes, got 2764640 bytes instead.
  <C++ Error>   Method/function failed.
  <C++ Source>  core/io/image.cpp:2227 @ initialize_data()
E 0:00:06:174   initialize_data: Expected Image data size of 1200x540x2 (RedGreen without mipmaps) = 1296000 bytes, got 1382239 bytes instead.
  <C++ Error>   Method/function failed.
  <C++ Source>  core/io/image.cpp:2227 @ initialize_data()
```
**Available Camera Formats (Logged from Device):**

title: Editor hang in RenderingDeviceDriverVulkan::fence_wait(), Win11, NVidia, Forward+
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.x, 4.5 stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7260) - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900K (32 threads) - 63.71 GiB memory

### Issue description

The editor randomly hangs, and if examined in the debugger, the top stack frame in the main thread is in `RenderingDeviceDriverVulkan::fence_wait()` [here](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/d7382aa05854df37c2f21aff02a693f8104fb95d/drivers/vulkan/rendering_device_driver_vulkan.cpp#L2549). The only solution is to kill the process and restart the editor.

### Steps to reproduce

I have not found a reliable way to trigger it, but when it does trigger, it is almost always immediately after opening or closing a popup window (e.g. color picker, rename text field).

This happens once every a few hours on average, but does not require the editor to have been running for any specific length of time - some days it doesn't happen at all, other days it happens every few minutes.

It may be relevant that I always have editor windows (main window, Inspector, FileSystem, etc.) on different monitors. I don't think it's relevant, but I only use Godot for 2D projects.

If relevant, I have G-SYNC enabled and v-sync set to "Use the 3D application setting" in the NVidia control panel.

I'm happy to investigate further if you have suggestions.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I was able to trigger it at least once in an empty project in Godot 4.5 (New Project, no scenes, no resources) with default settings.

author: Ramyssou

title: Jolt Physics job system sometimes exceeds the maximum number of allocated jobs
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable

### System information

Ryzen 9 9950X (from the original report)

### Issue description

Originally reported in jrouwe/JoltPhysics#1701 by @Temptica as:

> Jolt Physics job system exceeded the maximum number of jobs. This should not happen. Please report this. Waiting for jobs to become available...
> <C++ Source> modules/jolt_physics/spaces/jolt_job_system.cpp:125 @ CreateJob()
>
> It doesn't always happen, so currently I don't have a good idea of when it happens. But most likely when I load in a few items into the scene.

and:

> It sometimes happens when I load my scene, not always. I'm using a fixed seed which makes it weirder. The scene loads and works, though it's the shop which loads with a preview of the map, so there isn't much to notice from simulation wise.

This refers to the warning within our integration of the Jolt Physics job system, seen here:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/d7382aa05854df37c2f21aff02a693f8104fb95d/modules/jolt_physics/spaces/jolt_job_system.cpp#L125

The jobs are allocated from a fixed-size pool of 2048 jobs, using Jolt's own `JPH::FixedSizeFreeList`. The fixed-size pool is mainly to avoid heap allocations, but also serves as a sanity check, since there should never be a reason for this pool to be exceeded, unless something has gone wrong.

Unfortunately we have no ability to reproduce this yet, so it's unclear if this is an issue within our integration or with Jolt itself. If someone is able to share a reproduction project of some sort, that would be much appreciated.

### Steps to reproduce

None yet, unfortunately.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

None yet, unfortunately.

title: [4.5] Editor: the first tab break inside text exactly on the tabstop is invisible
body:
### Tested versions

4.5

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+)

### Issue description

In the GDScript editor, hitting _tab_ usually inserts a visible tab.
However, if the tab comes exactly on a tab stop (ie, at a multiple of four characters), and sits inside a word, then that tab is not displayed.

This can lead to some really confusing editor messages as GDScript can't parse the hidden whitespace. This can crop up a fair amount if the user's used to tab completion and hits tab too soon, and in the wrong spot.



### Steps to reproduce

Open a new GDScript script.

Type this:
```
var hello = "world"
```
Put the cursor before the `o`. Hit tab. The break is essentially invisible: if you're looking for it, the `o` moves by a pixel or two.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

n/a

title: Vector Tearing on Metallic Surfaces present in 4.5 but not 4.4
body: ### Tested versions Tested in Godot 4.5 stable vs Godot 4.4 stable. (4.4.1 not tested.) ### System information 2019 MBP, Mac OS 15.6.1, Intel CPU, Intel UHD Graphics 630 ### Issue description - Triangle edges have an "outline" on metallic ("shiny") surfaces. - Smooth shading seems require to cause the issue. - Setting Roughness all the way up makes them go away but that's removes the metallic effect. - The first frame of the running game does not have the problem. - The issue is present in 4.5 but not 4.4. (4.4.1 was not tested.) ### Steps to reproduce - Create a new file in Blender and press A then X then D to safely annihilate the default cube. - Press Shift + A and create a new Sphere Mesh. - Right click on the Mesh and select Shade Smooth. - Add a new Material. - Set Metallic to 0.5. - Set Roughness to 0.0. - Export the file and a GLB/GLTF. - Open Godot 4.5 stable. - Make a new 3D Scene and Place the GLB in the node tree. - You should immediately see the issue in 3D preview area, and when you run the Scene. If you add the same GLB to Godot 4.4 stable, you will not see the issue. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [vector_tearing_issue_2025-09-20_15-15-35.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22445271/vector_tearing_issue_2025-09-20_15-15-35.zip)

title: DisplayServer.tts_get_voices() crashes on Android without TTS
body:
### Tested versions

Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.official.49a5bc7b6

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.official.49a5bc7b6

### Issue description

On Android with TTS, everything works fine and there are no crashes.
On Android without TTS, calling DisplayServer.tts_get_voices() and DisplayServer.tts_get_voices_for_language("en") causes the application to crash.

I had to write an Android plugin in Android Studio to solve this problem and prevent the app from crashing.

```
package org.godotengine.plugin.android.checktts

import android.content.Intent
import android.speech.tts.TextToSpeech
import android.util.Log
import org.godotengine.godot.Godot
import org.godotengine.godot.plugin.GodotPlugin
import org.godotengine.godot.plugin.UsedByGodot

class GodotAndroidPlugin(godot: Godot): GodotPlugin(godot) {

    override fun getPluginName() = BuildConfig.GODOT_PLUGIN_NAME

    @UsedByGodot
    fun canUseTTS(): Boolean {
        return try {
            val checkIntent = Intent(TextToSpeech.Engine.ACTION_CHECK_TTS_DATA)
            val resolveInfo = activity?.packageManager?.queryIntentActivities(checkIntent, 0)
            val result = (resolveInfo?.size ?: 0) > 0
            Log.v(pluginName, "TTS available: $result")
            result
        } catch (e: Exception) {
            Log.e(pluginName, "TTS check error: ${e.message}")
            false
        }
    }
}
```
I haven't found any other way to solve this problem.




### Steps to reproduce

On Android without TTS, calling DisplayServer.tts_get_voices() and DisplayServer.tts_get_voices_for_language("en") causes the application to crash.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[MRP_TTS_bag.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22447730/MRP_TTS_bag.zip)

NodePath Property exported with usage hint `PROPERTY_USAGE_NODE_PATH_FROM_SCENE_ROOT` does not actually differ from normal NodePath property.

### Steps to reproduce

    - Export a (real) NodePath property, whose usage is set in `_validate_property` to `PROPERTY_USAGE_NODE_PATH_FROM_SCENE_ROOT`
- Add node class script to a Node in the scene

title: Using load() in multiple threads does not restore safety_checks
body:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.5.stable

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) 

### Issue description

Using `load()` or `ResourceLoader.load()` in thread will allow subsequent code to obtain nodes in the scene tree

If it is for performance or not a bug, I think we should add some explanation to the documentation.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/de950bc2-805d-4059-9025-af6f9c3926a0)

### Steps to reproduce

New Scene -> Add Script -> Add Child
```
extends Node2D

var thread

func _ready():
    thread = Thread.new()
    thread.start(thread1_function)

func thread1_function():
    ResourceLoader.load("res://icon.svg")
    print(JSON.stringify(get_children(), "\t"))
    Thread.set_thread_safety_checks_enabled(true)
    print(JSON.stringify(get_children(), "\t"))

func _exit_tree():
    thread.wait_to_finish()

```

author: WorldyBear

title: ProjectSettings `settings_changed` signal always fires twice
body:
### Tested versions

- Issue has been around since the `settings_changed` signal was introduced in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/c5ec847aa0230cd4fa090c2cbd1b1e9638a9253e
- Reproducible in 4.2.stable, 4.5.stable, main branch

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.6.1) - Single-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - dedicated AMD Radeon Pro 5500M - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9880H CPU @ 2.30GHz (16 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Whenever a project setting is changed, the ProjectSettings.settings_changed signal is triggered twice.

This has been around since the signal was introduced in 4.2

On further investigation, the first trigger is the actual setting change, the second is because the project features setting `application/config/features` is recalculated every time, and its value is set, and hence the signal is emitted, even if the old and new values are the same.
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/149a4b4ca12e2baf700020fc10f00d5b0a5e9f0d/core/config/project_settings.cpp#L1148

### Steps to reproduce

1. Download the MRP. It contains an empty project with a simple addon that listens to the project `ProjectSettings.settings_changed` signal
2. Go to Project Settings -> Plugins and enable the MRP plugin. You will see that this itself triggers the signal twice.
3. Once enabled, any project setting change will trigger the signal twice.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/47f36d21-a044-4370-a466-b887b919ef1e

The script in the MRP is as follows

``` gdscript
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

func _enter_tree() -> void:
	ProjectSettings.settings_changed.connect(_on_project_settings_changed)
	
func _on_project_settings_changed() -> void:
	print("_on_project_settings_changed")
	
func _exit_tree() -> void:
	ProjectSettings.settings_changed.disconnect(_on_project_settings_changed)

```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[double-signal.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22451948/double-signal.zip)

title: `LocalVector::reserve()` errors during collisions when using continuous collision detection with Jolt physics
body: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.5.stable _Not_ reproducible in 4.4.1.stable ### System information Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700F CPU @ 2.90GHz (16 threads) - 15.92 GiB memory ### Issue description When using Jolt physics, `RigidBody3D` nodes that have CCD (continuous collision detection) enabled may generate native errors in the console when they collide (it is pretty random, sometimes not generating any errors on a collision, but sometimes generating multiple per collision). But it is always _on a collision_, not passively. ### Steps to reproduce I don't have a reliable repro outside of my own project which is huge. What is known so far: - error only occurs during collisions - occurrence and number of errors is probabilistic - setting `continuous_cd` to `false` makes the error go away - setting `max_contacts_reported` to `0` makes the error go away - using the old Godot physics makes the error go away - setting `contact_monitor` to `false` makes no difference - using all default settings (in 3D, Common, and Jolt sections) does not make a difference I'm hoping these clues will be at least somewhat helpful in ringing a bell, but I will update with more details if I find any.🙏 ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

title: unused signals in abstract classes produce warning
body:
### Tested versions

v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]




### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm) 12 on X11 - X11 display driver, Single-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB (nvidia) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4770 CPU @ 3.40GHz (8 threads) - 7.67 GiB memory

### Issue description

Defining a signal in an abstract class (and not using it within that abstract class) results in a warning.

```
Line 5 (UNUSED_SIGNAL):The signal "my_signal" is declared but never explicitly used in the class.
```

The warning is accurate, however an abstract class is not typically a _complete_ implementation of itself.

#### Reason this is an issue

I want to define a signal in an abstract class, keep [DRY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Don%27t_repeat_yourself) and benefit from autocomplete when connecting to it. i.e.

```gdscript
@export var myClass: MyAbstractClass

func _ready():
    myClass.mySignal.connect(...)

```

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
@abstract
class_name MyClass
extends Resource

signal my_signal
```
```
Line 5 (UNUSED_SIGNAL):The signal "my_signal" is declared but never explicitly used in the class.
```
